18:14.transliteration: Warabatna AAalaquloobihim ith qamoo faqaloo rabbunarabbu assamawati wal-ardilan nadAAuwa min doonihi ilahan laqad qulna ithanshatata
18:14.translation: And We bound [i.e., made firm] their hearts when they stood up and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never will we invoke besides Him any deity. We would have certainly spoken, then, an excessive transgression.

18:15.transliteration: Haola-i qawmuna ittakhathoomin doonihi alihatan lawla ya/toona AAalayhim bisultaninbayyinin faman athlamu mimmani iftara AAalaAllahi kathiba
18:15.translation: These, our people, have taken besides Him deities. Why do they not bring for [worship of] them a clear evidence? And who is more unjust than one who invents about Allāh a lie?"

18:16.transliteration: Wa-ithi iAAtazaltumoohum wamayaAAbudoona illa Allaha fa/woo ila alkahfiyanshur lakum rabbukum min rahmatihi wayuhayyi/ lakum minamrikum mirfaqa
18:16.translation: [The youths said to one another], "And when you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship other than Allāh, retreat to the cave. Your Lord will spread out for you of His mercy and will prepare for you from your affair facility."

18:17.transliteration: Watara ashshamsa itha talaAAattazawaru AAan kahfihim thata alyameeni wa-ithagharabat taqriduhum thata ashshimaliwahum fee fajwatin minhu thalika min ayatiAllahi man yahdi Allahu fahuwa almuhtadi waman yudlilfalan tajida lahu waliyyan murshida
18:17.translation: And [had you been present], you would see the sun when it rose, inclining away from their cave on the right, and when it set, passing away from them on the left, while they were [lying] within an open space thereof. That was from the signs of Allāh. He whom Allāh guides is the [rightly] guided, but he whom He sends astray - never will you find for him a protecting guide.

18:18.transliteration: Watahsabuhum ayqathanwahum ruqoodun wanuqallibuhum thata alyameeni wathataashshimali wakalbuhum basitun thiraAAayhibilwaseedi lawi ittalaAAta AAalayhimlawallayta minhum firaran walamuli/ta minhum ruAAba
18:18.translation: And you would think them awake, while they were asleep. And We turned them to the right and to the left, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance. If you had looked at them, you would have turned from them in flight and been filled by them with terror.

18:19.transliteration: Wakathalika baAAathnahumliyatasaaloo baynahum qala qa-ilun minhumkam labithtum qaloo labithna yawman aw baAAdayawmin qaloo rabbukum aAAlamu bima labithtum fabAAathooahadakum biwariqikum hathihi ila almadeenatifalyanthur ayyuha azka taAAamanfalya/tikum birizqin minhu walyatalattaf walayushAAiranna bikum ahada
18:19.translation: And similarly, We awakened them that they might question one another. Said a speaker from among them, "How long have you remained [here]?" They said, "We have remained a day or part of a day." They said, "Your Lord is most knowing of how long you remained. So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look to which is the best of food and bring you provision from it and let him be cautious. And let no one be aware of you.

18:20.transliteration: Innahum in yathharoo AAalaykumyarjumookum aw yuAAeedookum fee millatihim walan tuflihooithan abada
18:20.translation: Indeed, if they come to know of you, they will stone you or return you to their religion. And never would you succeed, then - ever."

18:21.transliteration: Wakathalika aAAtharnaAAalayhim liyaAAlamoo anna waAAda Allahi haqqunwaanna asaAAata la rayba feeha ithyatanazaAAoona baynahum amrahum faqaloo ibnooAAalayhim bunyanan rabbuhum aAAlamu bihim qala allatheenaghalaboo AAala amrihim lanattakhithanna AAalayhimmasjida
18:21.translation: And similarly, We caused them to be found that they [who found them] would know that the promise of Allāh is truth and that of the Hour there is no doubt. [That was] when they disputed among themselves about their affair and [then] said, "Construct over them a structure. Their Lord is most knowing about them." Said those who prevailed in the matter, "We will surely take [for ourselves] over them a masjid."

18:22.transliteration: Sayaqooloona thalathatun rabiAAuhumkalbuhum wayaqooloona khamsatun sadisuhum kalbuhum rajmanbilghaybi wayaqooloona sabAAatun wathaminuhumkalbuhum qul rabbee aAAlamu biAAiddatihim ma yaAAlamuhumilla qaleelun fala tumari feehim illamiraan thahiran wala tastaftifeehim minhum ahada
18:22.translation: They [i.e., people] will say there were three, the fourth of them being their dog; and they will say there were five, the sixth of them being their dog - guessing at the unseen; and they will say there were seven, and the eighth of them was their dog. Say, [O Muḥammad], "My Lord is most knowing of their number. None knows them except a few. So do not argue about them except with an obvious argument and do not inquire about them among [the speculators] from anyone."

18:23.transliteration: Wala taqoolanna lishay-in innee faAAilunthalika ghada
18:23.translation: And never say of anything, "Indeed, I will do that tomorrow,"

18:24.transliteration: Illa an yashaa Allahuwathkur rabbaka itha naseeta waqul AAasaan yahdiyani rabbee li-aqraba min hatha rashada
18:24.translation: Except [when adding], "If Allāh wills." And remember your Lord when you forget [it] and say, "Perhaps my Lord will guide me to what is nearer than this to right conduct."

18:25.transliteration: Walabithoo fee kahfihim thalathami-atin sineena wazdadoo tisAAa
18:25.translation: And they remained in their cave for three hundred years and exceeded by nine.

18:26.transliteration: Quli Allahu aAAlamu bimalabithoo lahu ghaybu assamawati wal-ardiabsir bihi waasmiAA ma lahum min doonihi minwaliyyin wala yushriku fee hukmihi ahada
18:26.translation: Say, "Allāh is most knowing of how long they remained. He has [knowledge of] the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth. How Seeing is He and how Hearing! They have not besides Him any protector, and He shares not His legislation with anyone."

18:27.transliteration: Watlu ma oohiya ilaykamin kitabi rabbika la mubaddila likalimatihiwalan tajida min doonihi multahada
18:27.translation: And recite, [O Muḥammad], what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord. There is no changer of His words, and never will you find in other than Him a refuge.

18:28.transliteration: Wasbir nafsaka maAAa allatheenayadAAoona rabbahum bilghadati walAAashiyyiyureedoona wajhahu wala taAAdu AAaynaka AAanhumtureedu zeenata alhayati addunya walatutiAA man aghfalna qalbahu AAan thikrinawattabaAAa hawahu wakana amruhu furuta
18:28.translation: And keep yourself patient [by being] with those who call upon their Lord in the morning and the evening, seeking His face [i.e., acceptance]. And let not your eyes pass beyond them, desiring adornments of the worldly life, and do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance and who follows his desire and whose affair is ever [in] neglect.

18:29.transliteration: Waquli alhaqqu min rabbikum faman shaafalyu/min waman shaa falyakfur inna aAAtadnaliththalimeena naran ahatabihim suradiquha wa-in yastagheethoo yughathoobima-in kalmuhli yashwee alwujooha bi/sa ashsharabuwasaat murtafaqa
18:29.translation: And say, "The truth is from your Lord, so whoever wills - let him believe; and whoever wills - let him disbelieve." Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire whose walls will surround them. And if they call for relief, they will be relieved with water like murky oil, which scalds [their] faces. Wretched is the drink, and evil is the resting place.

18:30.transliteration: Inna allatheena amanoowaAAamiloo assalihati inna lanudeeAAu ajra man ahsana AAamala
18:30.translation: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - indeed, We will not allow to be lost the reward of any who did well in deeds.

18:31.transliteration: Ola-ika lahum jannatu AAadnintajree min tahtihimu al-anharu yuhallawnafeeha min asawira min thahabin waylbasoonathiyaban khudran min sundusin wa-istabraqinmuttaki-eena feeha AAala al-ara-iki niAAma aththawabuwahasunat murtafaqa
18:31.translation: Those will have gardens of perpetual residence; beneath them rivers will flow. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and will wear green garments of fine silk and brocade, reclining therein on adorned couches. Excellent is the reward, and good is the resting place.

18:32.transliteration: Wadrib lahum mathalanrajulayni jaAAalna li-ahadihima jannataynimin aAAnabin wahafafnahuma binakhlinwajaAAalna baynahuma zarAAa
18:32.translation: And present to them an example of two men: We granted to one of them two gardens of grapevines, and We bordered them with palm trees and placed between them [fields of] crops.

18:33.transliteration: Kilta aljannatayni atat okulahawalam tathlim minhu shay-an wafajjarna khilalahumanahara
18:33.translation: Each of the two gardens produced its fruit and did not fall short thereof in anything. And We caused to gush forth within them a river.

18:34.transliteration: Wakana lahu thamarun faqala lisahibihiwahuwa yuhawiruhu ana aktharu minka malanwaaAAazzu nafara
18:34.translation: And he had fruit, so he said to his companion while he was conversing with him, "I am greater than you in wealth and mightier in [numbers of] men."

18:35.transliteration: Wadakhala jannatahu wahuwa thalimunlinafsihi qala ma athunnu an tabeedahathihi abada
18:35.translation: And he entered his garden while he was unjust to himself. He said, "I do not think that this will perish - ever.

18:36.transliteration: Wama athunnu assaAAataqa-imatan wala-in rudidtu ila rabbee laajidannakhayran minha munqalaba
18:36.translation: And I do not think the Hour will occur. And even if I should be brought back to my Lord, I will surely find better than this as a return."

18:37.transliteration: Qala lahu sahibuhu wahuwa yuhawiruhuakafarta billathee khalaqaka min turabinthumma min nutfatin thumma sawwaka rajula
18:37.translation: His companion said to him while he was conversing with him, "Have you disbelieved in He who created you from dust and then from a sperm-drop and then proportioned you [as] a man?

18:38.transliteration: Lakinna huwa Allahu rabbee walaoshriku birabbee ahada
18:38.translation: But as for me, He is Allāh, my Lord, and I do not associate with my Lord anyone.

18:39.transliteration: Walawla ith dakhalta jannatakaqulta ma shaa Allahu la quwwata illabillahi in tarani ana aqalla minka malanwawalada
18:39.translation: And why did you, when you entered your garden, not say, 'What Allāh willed [has occurred]; there is no power except in Allāh'? Although you see me less than you in wealth and children,

18:40.transliteration: FaAAasa rabbee an yu/tiyani khayranmin jannatika wayursila AAalayha husbananmina assama-i fatusbiha saAAeedanzalaqa
18:40.translation: It may be that my Lord will give me [something] better than your garden and will send upon it a [disastrous] penalty from the sky, and it will become a smooth, dusty ground,

18:41.transliteration: Aw yusbiha maohaghawran falan tastateeAAa lahu talaba
18:41.translation: Or its water will become sunken [into the earth], so you would never be able to seek it."

18:42.transliteration: Waoheeta bithamarihi faasbahayuqallibu kaffayhi AAala ma anfaqa feehawahiya khawiyatun AAala AAurooshihawayaqoolu ya laytanee lam oshrik birabbee ahada
18:42.translation: And his fruits were encompassed [by ruin], so he began to turn his hands about [in dismay] over what he had spent on it, while it had collapsed upon its trellises, and said, "Oh, I wish I had not associated with my Lord anyone."

18:43.transliteration: Walam takun lahu fi-atun yansuroonahumin dooni Allahi wama kana muntasira
18:43.translation: And there was for him no company to aid him other than Allāh, nor could he defend himself.

18:44.transliteration: Hunalika alwalayatu lillahialhaqqi huwa khayrun thawaban wakhayrun AAuqba
18:44.translation: There the authority is [completely] for Allāh, the Truth. He is best in reward and best in outcome.

18:45.transliteration: Wadrib lahum mathala alhayatiaddunya kama-in anzalnahu mina assama-ifakhtalata bihi nabatu al-ardi faasbahahasheeman tathroohu arriyahu wakanaAllahu AAala kulli shay-in muqtadira
18:45.translation: And present to them the example of the life of this world, [its being] like rain which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mingles with it and [then] it becomes dry remnants, scattered by the winds. And Allāh is ever, over all things, Perfect in Ability.

18:46.transliteration: Almalu walbanoona zeenatu alhayatiaddunya walbaqiyatu assalihatukhayrun AAinda rabbika thawaban wakhayrun amala
18:46.translation: Wealth and children are [but] adornment of the worldly life. But the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for [one's] hope.

18:47.transliteration: Wayawma nusayyiru aljibala wataraal-arda barizatan wahasharnahum falamnughadir minhum ahada
18:47.translation: And [warn of] the Day when We will remove the mountains and you will see the earth exposed, and We will gather them and not leave behind from them anyone.

18:48.transliteration: WaAAuridoo AAala rabbika saffanlaqad ji/tumoona kama khalaqnakum awwalamarratin bal zaAAamtum allan najAAala lakum mawAAida
18:48.translation: And they will be presented before your Lord in rows, [and He will say], "You have certainly come to Us just as We created you the first time. But you claimed that We would never make for you an appointment."

18:49.transliteration: WawudiAAa alkitabu fataraalmujrimeena mushfiqeena mimma feehi wayaqooloona yawaylatana ma lihatha alkitabi layughadiru sagheeratan wala kabeeratan illaahsaha wawajadoo ma AAamiloo hadiranwala yathlimu rabbuka ahada
18:49.translation: And the record [of deeds] will be placed [open], and you will see the criminals fearful of that within it, and they will say, "Oh, woe to us! What is this book that leaves nothing small or great except that it has enumerated it?" And they will find what they did present [before them]. And your Lord does injustice to no one.

18:50.transliteration: Wa-ith qulna lilmala-ikatiosjudoo li-adama fasajadoo illa ibleesa kanamina aljinni fafasaqa AAan amri rabbihi afatattakhithoonahuwathurriyyatahu awliyaa min doonee wahum lakumAAaduwwun bi/sa liththalimeena badala
18:50.translation: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except for Iblees. He was of the jinn and departed from [i.e., disobeyed] the command of his Lord. Then will you take him and his descendants as allies other than Me while they are enemies to you? Wretched it is for the wrongdoers as an exchange.

18:51.transliteration: Ma ashhadtuhum khalqa assamawatiwal-ardi wala khalqa anfusihim wamakuntu muttakhitha almudilleena AAaduda
18:51.translation: I did not make them witness to the creation of the heavens and the earth or to the creation of themselves, and I would not have taken the misguiders as assistants.

18:52.transliteration: Wayawma yaqoolu nadoo shuraka-iyaallatheena zaAAamtum fadaAAawhum falam yastajeeboo lahumwajaAAalna baynahum mawbiqa
18:52.translation: And [warn of] the Day when He will say, "Call My 'partners' whom you claimed," and they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them. And We will put between them [a valley of] destruction.

18:53.transliteration: Waraa almujrimoona annarafathannoo annahum muwaqiAAooha walamyajidoo AAanha masrifa
18:53.translation: And the criminals will see the Fire and will be certain that they are to fall therein. And they will not find from it a way elsewhere.

18:54.transliteration: Walaqad sarrafna fee hathaalqur-ani linnasi min kulli mathalin wakanaal-insanu akthara shay-in jadala
18:54.translation: And We have certainly diversified in this Qur’ān for the people from every [kind of] example; but man has ever been, most of anything, [prone to] dispute.

18:55.transliteration: Wama manaAAa annasa anyu/minoo ith jaahumu alhuda wayastaghfiroorabbahum illa an ta/tiyahum sunnatu al-awwaleena awya/tiyahumu alAAathabu qubula
18:55.translation: And nothing has prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them and from asking forgiveness of their Lord except that there [must] befall them the [accustomed] precedent of the former peoples or that the punishment should come [directly] before them.

18:56.transliteration: Wama nursilu almursaleena illamubashshireena wamunthireena wayujadilu allatheenakafaroo bilbatili liyudhidoo bihi alhaqqawattakhathoo ayatee wama onthiroohuzuwa
18:56.translation: And We send not the messengers except as bringers of good tidings and warners. And those who disbelieve dispute by [using] falsehood to [attempt to] invalidate thereby the truth and have taken My verses, and that of which they are warned, in ridicule.

18:57.transliteration: Waman athlamu mimman thukkirabi-ayati rabbihi faaAArada AAanhawanasiya ma qaddamat yadahu inna jaAAalnaAAala quloobihim akinnatan an yafqahoohu wafee athanihimwaqran wa-in tadAAuhum ila alhuda falan yahtadoo ithanabada
18:57.translation: And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord but turns away from them and forgets what his hands have put forth? Indeed, We have placed over their hearts coverings, lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness. And if you invite them to guidance - they will never be guided, then - ever.

18:58.transliteration: Warabbuka alghafooru thoo arrahmatilaw yu-akhithuhum bima kasaboo laAAajjalalahumu alAAathaba bal lahum mawAAidun lan yajidoo mindoonihi maw-ila
18:58.translation: And your Lord is the Forgiving, the possessor of mercy. If He were to impose blame upon them for what they earned, He would have hastened for them the punishment. Rather, for them is an appointment from which they will never find an escape.

18:59.transliteration: Watilka alqura ahlaknahum lammathalamoo wajaAAalna limahlikihim mawAAida
18:59.translation: And those cities - We destroyed them when they wronged, and We made for their destruction an appointed time.

18:60.transliteration: Wa-ith qala moosa lifatahula abrahu hatta ablugha majmaAAa albahrayniaw amdiya huquba
18:60.translation: And [mention] when Moses said to his boy [i.e., servant], "I will not cease [traveling] until I reach the junction of the two seas or continue for a long period."

18:61.transliteration: Falamma balagha majmaAAabaynihima nasiya hootahuma fattakhathasabeelahu fee albahri saraba
18:61.translation: But when they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, and it took its course into the sea, slipping away.

18:62.transliteration: Falamma jawaza qalalifatahu atina ghadaana laqadlaqeena min safarina hatha nasaba
18:62.translation: So when they had passed beyond it, [Moses] said to his boy, "Bring us our morning meal. We have certainly suffered in this, our journey, [much] fatigue."

18:63.transliteration: Qala araayta ith awaynaila assakhrati fa-innee naseetu alhootawama ansaneehu illa ashshaytanuan athkurahu wattakhatha sabeelahu fee albahriAAajaba
18:63.translation: He said, "Did you see when we retired to the rock? Indeed, I forgot [there] the fish. And none made me forget it except Satan - that I should mention it. And it took its course into the sea amazingly."

18:64.transliteration: Qala thalika ma kunnanabghi fartadda AAala atharihimaqasasa
18:64.translation: [Moses] said, "That is what we were seeking." So they returned, following their footprints.

18:65.transliteration: Fawajada AAabdan min AAibadinaataynahu rahmatan min AAindinawaAAallamnahu min ladunna AAilma
18:65.translation: And they found a servant from among Our servants [i.e., al-Khiḍr] to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge.

18:66.transliteration: Qala lahu moosa halattabiAAuka AAala an tuAAallimani mimma AAullimtarushda
18:66.translation: Moses said to him, "May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgement?"

18:67.translation: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

18:68.transliteration: Wakayfa tasbiru AAala malam tuhit bihi khubra
18:68.translation: And how can you have patience for what you do not encompass in knowledge?"

18:69.transliteration: Qala satajidunee in shaa Allahusabiran wala aAAsee laka amra
18:69.translation: [Moses] said, "You will find me, if Allāh wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in [any] order."

18:70.transliteration: Qala fa-ini ittabaAAtanee falatas-alnee AAan shay-in hatta ohditha lakaminhu thikra
18:70.translation: He said, "Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention [i.e., explanation]."

18:71.transliteration: Fantalaqa hattaitha rakiba fee assafeenati kharaqahaqala akharaqtaha litughriqa ahlaha laqadji/ta shay-an imra
18:71.translation: So they set out, until when they had embarked on the ship, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] tore it open. [Moses] said, "Have you torn it open to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing."

18:72.transliteration: Qala alam aqul innaka lan tastateeAAamaAAiya sabra
18:72.translation: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "Did I not say that with me you would never be able to have patience?"

18:73.transliteration: Qala la tu-akhithneebima naseetu wala turhiqnee min amree AAusra
18:73.translation: [Moses] said, "Do not blame me for what I forgot and do not overwhelm me in my matter with difficulty."

18:74.transliteration: Fantalaqa hattaitha laqiya ghulaman faqatalahu qalaaqatalta nafsan zakiyyatan bighayri nafsin laqad ji/ta shay-annukra
18:74.translation: So they set out, until when they met a boy, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] killed him. [Moses] said, "Have you killed a pure soul for other than [having killed] a soul? You have certainly done a deplorable thing."

18:75.transliteration: Qala alam aqul laka innaka lan tastateeAAamaAAiya sabra
18:75.translation: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "Did I not tell you that with me you would never be able to have patience?"

18:76.transliteration: Qala in saaltuka AAan shay-in baAAdahafala tusahibnee qad balaghta min ladunnee AAuthra
18:76.translation: [Moses] said, "If I should ask you about anything after this, then do not keep me as a companion. You have obtained from me an excuse."

18:77.transliteration: Fantalaqa hattaitha ataya ahla qaryatin istatAAamaahlaha faabaw an yudayyifoohuma fawajadafeeha jidaran yureedu an yanqadda faaqamahuqala law shi/ta lattakhathta AAalayhi ajra
18:77.translation: So they set out, until when they came to the people of a town, they asked its people for food, but they refused to offer them hospitality. And they found therein a wall about to collapse, so he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] restored it. [Moses] said, "If you wished, you could have taken for it a payment."

18:78.transliteration: Qala hatha firaqubaynee wabaynika saonabbi-oka bita/weeli ma lam tastatiAAAAalayhi sabra
18:78.translation: [Al-Khiḍr] said, "This is parting between me and you. I will inform you of the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience.

18:79.transliteration: Amma assafeenatu fakanatlimasakeena yaAAmaloona fee albahri faaradtu anaAAeebaha wakana waraahum malikun ya/khuthukulla safeenatin ghasba
18:79.translation: As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.

18:80.transliteration: Waamma alghulamu fakanaabawahu mu/minayni fakhasheena an yurhiqahumatughyanan wakufra
18:80.translation: And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief.

18:81.transliteration: Faaradna an yubdilahumarabbuhuma khayran minhu zakatan waaqraba ruhma
18:81.translation: So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.

18:82.transliteration: Waamma aljidaru fakanalighulamayni yateemayni fee almadeenati wakana tahtahukanzun lahuma wakana aboohuma salihanfaarada rabbuka an yablugha ashuddahumawayastakhrija kanzahuma rahmatan min rabbikawama faAAaltuhu AAan amree thalika ta/weelu malam tastiAA AAalayhi sabra
18:82.translation: And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and there was beneath it a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous. So your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord. And I did it not of my own accord. That is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience."

18:83.transliteration: Wayas-aloonaka AAan thee alqarnayniqul saatloo AAalaykum minhu thikra
18:83.translation: And they ask you, [O Muḥammad], about Dhul-Qarnayn. Say, "I will recite to you about him a report."

18:84.transliteration: Inna makkanna lahu fee al-ardiwaataynahu min kulli shay-in sababa
18:84.translation: Indeed, We established him upon the earth, and We gave him from everything a way [i.e., means].

18:86.transliteration: Hatta itha balaghamaghriba ashshamsi wajadaha taghrubu fee AAaynin hami-atinwawajada AAindaha qawman qulna ya thaalqarnayni imma an tuAAaththiba wa-imma antattakhitha feehim husna
18:86.translation: Until, when he reached the setting of the sun [i.e., the west], he found it [as if] setting in a body of dark water, and he found near it a people. We [i.e., Allāh] said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, either you punish [them] or else adopt among them [a way of] goodness."

18:87.transliteration: Qala amma man thalamafasawfa nuAAaththibuhu thumma yuraddu ila rabbihifayuAAaththibuhu AAathaban nukra
18:87.translation: He said, "As for one who wrongs, we will punish him. Then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment [i.e., Hellfire].

18:88.transliteration: Waamma man amana waAAamila salihanfalahu jazaan alhusna wasanaqoolu lahu minamrina yusra
18:88.translation: But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of the best [i.e., Paradise], and we [i.e., Dhul-Qarnayn] will speak to him from our command with ease."

18:90.transliteration: Hatta itha balagha matliAAaashshamsi wajadaha tatluAAu AAalaqawmin lam najAAal lahum min dooniha sitra
18:90.translation: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

18:91.translation: Thus. And We had encompassed [all] that he had in knowledge.

18:93.transliteration: Hatta itha balaghabayna assaddayni wajada min doonihima qawman layakadoona yafqahoona qawla
18:93.translation: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.

18:94.transliteration: Qaloo ya tha alqarnayniinna ya/jooja wama/jooja mufsidoona fee al-ardi fahalnajAAalu laka kharjan AAala an tajAAala baynanawabaynahum sadda
18:94.translation: They said, "O Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are [great] corrupters in the land. So may we assign for you an expenditure that you might make between us and them a barrier?"

18:95.transliteration: Qala ma makkannee feehi rabbeekhayrun faaAAeenoonee biquwwatin ajAAal baynakum wabaynahum radma
18:95.translation: He said, "That in which my Lord has established me is better [than what you offer], but assist me with strength [i.e., manpower]; I will make between you and them a dam.

18:96.transliteration: Atoonee zubara alhadeedi hattaitha sawa bayna asadafayni qalaonfukhoo hatta itha jaAAalahu naran qalaatoonee ofrigh AAalayhi qitra
18:96.translation: Bring me bars of iron" - until, when he had leveled [them] between the two mountain walls, he said, "Blow [with bellows]," until when he had made it [like] fire, he said, "Bring me, that I may pour over it molten copper."

18:97.transliteration: Fama istaAAoo an yathharoohuwama istataAAoo lahu naqba
18:97.translation: So they [i.e., Gog and Magog] were unable to pass over it, nor were they able [to effect] in it any penetration.

18:98.transliteration: Qala hatha rahmatun minrabbee fa-itha jaa waAAdu rabbee jaAAalahu dakkaawakana waAAdu rabbee haqqa
18:98.translation: [Dhul-Qarnayn] said, "This is a mercy from my Lord; but when the promise of my Lord comes [i.e., approaches], He will make it level, and ever is the promise of my Lord true."

18:99.transliteration: Watarakna baAAdahum yawma-ithinyamooju fee baAAdin wanufikha fee assoorifajamaAAnahum jamAAa
18:99.translation: And We will leave them that day surging over each other, and [then] the Horn will be blown, and We will assemble them in [one] assembly.

18:100.translation: And We will present Hell that Day to the disbelievers, on display -

18:101.transliteration: Allatheena kanat aAAyunuhumfee ghita-in AAan thikree wakanoo layastateeAAoona samAAa
18:101.translation: Those whose eyes had been within a cover [removed] from My remembrance, and they were not able to hear.

18:102.transliteration: Afahasiba allatheena kafarooan yattakhithoo AAibadee min doonee awliyaainna aAAtadna jahannama lilkafireena nuzula
18:102.translation: Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants instead of Me as allies? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.

18:103.translation: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Shall we [believers] inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds?

18:104.transliteration: Allatheena dalla saAAyuhumfee alhayati addunya wahum yahsaboonaannahum yuhsinoona sunAAa
18:104.translation: [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work."

18:105.transliteration: Ola-ika allatheena kafaroobi-ayati rabbihim waliqa-ihi fahabitataAAmaluhum fala nuqeemu lahum yawma alqiyamatiwazna
18:105.translation: Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in [their] meeting Him, so their deeds have become worthless; and We will not assign to them on the Day of Resurrection any weight [i.e., importance].

18:106.transliteration: Thalika jazaohum jahannamubima kafaroo wattakhathoo ayateewarusulee huzuwa
18:106.translation: That is their recompense - Hell - for what they denied and [because] they took My signs and My messengers in ridicule.

18:107.transliteration: Inna allatheena amanoowaAAamiloo assalihati kanat lahumjannatu alfirdawsi nuzula
18:107.translation: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - they will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging,

18:108.translation: Wherein they abide eternally. They will not desire from it any transfer.

18:109.transliteration: Qul law kana albahru midadanlikalimati rabbee lanafida albahru qabla an tanfadakalimatu rabbee walaw ji/na bimithlihi madada
18:109.translation: Say, "If the sea were ink for [writing] the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it in [continual] supplement."

18:110.transliteration: Qul innama ana basharunmithlukum yooha ilayya annama ilahukum ilahunwahidun faman kana yarjoo liqaa rabbihifalyaAAmal AAamalan salihan wala yushrikbiAAibadati rabbihi ahada
18:110.translation: Say, "I am only a man like you, to whom has been revealed that your god is one God. So whoever would hope for the meeting with his Lord - let him do righteous work and not associate in the worship of his Lord anyone."

19:2.translation: [This is] a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah

19:3.translation: When he called to his Lord a private call [i.e., supplication].

19:4.transliteration: Qala rabbi innee wahana alAAathmuminnee washtaAAala arra/su shayban walam akunbiduAAa-ika rabbi shaqiyya
19:4.translation: He said, "My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white, and never have I been in my supplication to You, my Lord, unhappy [i.e., disappointed].

19:5.transliteration: Wa-innee khiftu almawaliya min wara-eewakanati imraatee AAaqiran fahab lee min ladunkawaliyya
19:5.translation: And indeed, I fear the successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so give me from Yourself an heir

19:6.transliteration: Yarithunee wayarithu min ali yaAAqoobawajAAalhu rabbi radiyya
19:6.translation: Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]."

19:7.transliteration: Ya zakariyya innanubashshiruka bighulamin ismuhu yahya lamnajAAal lahu min qablu samiyya
19:7.translation: [He was told], "O Zechariah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be John. We have not assigned to any before [this] name."

19:8.transliteration: Qala rabbi anna yakoonu leeghulamun wakanati imraatee AAaqiran waqadbalaghtu mina alkibari AAitiyya
19:8.translation: He said, "My Lord, how will I have a boy when my wife has been barren and I have reached extreme old age?"

19:9.transliteration: Qala kathalika qalarabbuka huwa AAalayya hayyinun waqad khalaqtuka min qablu walamtaku shay-a
19:9.translation: [An angel] said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, for I created you before, while you were nothing.'"

19:10.transliteration: Qala rabbi ijAAal lee ayatan qalaayatuka alla tukallima annasa thalathalayalin sawiyya
19:10.translation: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound."

19:11.transliteration: Fakharaja AAala qawmihi mina almihrabifaawha ilayhim an sabbihoo bukratan waAAashiyya
19:11.translation: So he came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt [Allāh] in the morning and afternoon.

19:12.transliteration: Ya yahya khuthialkitaba biquwwatin waataynahu alhukmasabiyya
19:12.translation: [Allāh said], "O John, take the Scripture [i.e., adhere to it] with determination." And We gave him judgement [while yet] a boy

19:13.translation: And affection from Us and purity, and he was fearing of Allāh

19:14.translation: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not a disobedient tyrant.

19:15.transliteration: Wasalamun AAalayhi yawma wulidawayawma yamootu wayawma yubAAathu hayya
19:15.translation: And peace be upon him the day he was born and the day he dies and the day he is raised alive.

19:16.transliteration: Wathkur fee alkitabimaryama ithi intabathat min ahliha makanansharqiyya
19:16.translation: And mention, [O Muḥammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.

19:17.transliteration: Fattakhathat min doonihim hijabanfaarsalna ilayha roohanafatamaththala laha basharan sawiyya
19:17.translation: And she took, in seclusion from them, a screen. Then We sent to her Our Angel [i.e., Gabriel], and he represented himself to her as a well-proportioned man.

19:18.transliteration: Qalat innee aAAoothu birrahmaniminka in kunta taqiyya
19:18.translation: She said, "Indeed, I seek refuge in the Most Merciful from you, [so leave me], if you should be fearing of Allāh."

19:19.transliteration: Qala innama ana rasoolurabbiki li-ahaba laki ghulaman zakiyya
19:19.translation: He said, "I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you [news of] a pure boy [i.e., son]."

19:20.transliteration: Qalat anna yakoonu lee ghulamunwalam yamsasnee basharun walam aku baghiyya
19:20.translation: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste?"

19:21.transliteration: Qala kathaliki qalarabbuki huwa AAalayya hayyinun walinajAAalahu ayatan linnasiwarahmatan minna wakana amran maqdiyya
19:21.translation: He said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter [already] decreed.'"

19:22.translation: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.

19:23.transliteration: Faajaaha almakhadu ilajithAAi annakhlati qalat ya laytaneemittu qabla hatha wakuntu nasyan mansiyya
19:23.translation: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten."

19:24.transliteration: Fanadaha min tahtihaalla tahzanee qad jaAAala rabbuki tahtakisariyya
19:24.translation: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream.

19:25.transliteration: Wahuzzee ilayki bijithAAi annakhlatitusaqit AAalayki rutaban janiyya
19:25.translation: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates.

19:26.transliteration: Fakulee washrabee waqarree AAaynanfa-imma tarayinna mina albashari ahadan faqooleeinnee nathartu lirrahmani sawmanfalan okallima alyawma insiyya
19:26.translation: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Most Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to [any] man.'"

19:27.transliteration: Faatat bihi qawmaha tahmiluhuqaloo ya maryamu laqad ji/ti shay-an fariyya
19:27.translation: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented.

19:28.transliteration: Ya okhta haroona ma kanaabooki imraa saw-in wama kanat ommuki baghiyya
19:28.translation: O sister [i.e., descendant] of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste."

19:29.transliteration: Faasharat ilayhi qaloo kayfanukallimu man kana fee almahdi sabiyya
19:29.translation: So she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child?"

19:30.transliteration: Qala innee AAabdu Allahi ataniyaalkitaba wajaAAalanee nabiyya
19:30.translation: [Jesus] said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allāh. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet.

19:31.transliteration: WajaAAalanee mubarakan aynamakuntu waawsanee bissalati wazzakatima dumtu hayya
19:31.translation: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakāh as long as I remain alive

19:32.translation: And [made me] dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant.

19:33.transliteration: Wassalamu AAalayya yawmawulidtu wayawma amootu wayawma obAAathu hayya
19:33.translation: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive."

19:34.transliteration: Thalika AAeesa ibnu maryamaqawla alhaqqi allathee feehi yamtaroon
19:34.translation: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute.

19:35.transliteration: Ma kana lillahi anyattakhitha min waladin subhanahu itha qadaamran fa-innama yaqoolu lahu kun fayakoon
19:35.translation: It is not [befitting] for Allāh to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is.

19:36.transliteration: Wa-inna Allaha rabbee warabbukum faAAbudoohuhatha siratun mustaqeem
19:36.translation: [Jesus said], "And indeed, Allāh is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path."

19:37.transliteration: Fakhtalafa al-ahzabumin baynihim fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo min mashhadiyawmin AAatheem
19:37.translation: Then the factions differed [concerning Jesus] from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day.

19:38.transliteration: AsmiAA bihim waabsir yawma ya/toonanalakini aththalimoona alyawmafee dalalin mubeen
19:38.translation: How [clearly] they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error.

19:39.transliteration: Waanthirhum yawma alhasrati ithqudiya al-amru wahum fee ghaflatin wahum layu/minoon
19:39.translation: And warn them, [O Muḥammad], of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and [yet], they are in [a state of] heedlessness, and they do not believe.

19:40.transliteration: Inna nahnu narithu al-ardawaman AAalayha wa-ilayna yurjaAAoon
19:40.translation: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned.

19:41.transliteration: Wathkur fee alkitabiibraheema innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyya
19:41.translation: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.

19:42.transliteration: Ith qala li-abeehi yaabati lima taAAbudu ma la yasmaAAu wala yubsiruwala yughnee AAanka shay-a
19:42.translation: [Mention] when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all?

19:43.transliteration: Ya abati innee qad jaanee minaalAAilmi ma lam ya/tika fattabiAAnee ahdika siratansawiyya
19:43.translation: O my father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path.

19:44.transliteration: Ya abati la taAAbudi ashshaytanainna ashshaytana kana lirrahmaniAAasiyya
19:44.translation: O my father, do not worship [i.e., obey] Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Most Merciful, disobedient.

19:45.transliteration: Ya abati innee akhafu anyamassaka AAathabun mina arrahmanifatakoona lishshyatani waliyya
19:45.translation: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Most Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion [in Hellfire]."

19:46.transliteration: Qala araghibun anta AAan alihateeya ibraheemu la-in lam tantahi laarjumannaka wahjurneemaliyya
19:46.translation: [His father] said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time."

19:47.transliteration: Qala salamun AAalaykasaastaghfiru laka rabbee innahu kana bee hafiyya
19:47.translation: [Abraham] said, "Peace [i.e., safety] will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me.

19:48.transliteration: WaaAAtazilukum wama tadAAoona mindooni Allahi waadAAoo rabbee AAasa allaakoona biduAAa-i rabbee shaqiyya
19:48.translation: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allāh and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy [i.e., disappointed]."

19:49.transliteration: Falamma iAAtazalahum wamayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi wahabna lahu ishaqawayaAAqooba wakullan jaAAalna nabiyya
19:49.translation: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allāh, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet.

19:50.transliteration: Wawahabna lahum min rahmatinawajaAAalna lahum lisana sidqin AAaliyya
19:50.translation: And We gave them of Our mercy, and We made for them a mention [i.e., reputation] of high honor.

19:51.transliteration: Wathkur fee alkitabimoosa innahu kana mukhlasan wakanarasoolan nabiyya
19:51.translation: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.

19:52.transliteration: Wanadaynahu min janibiattoori al-aymani waqarrabnahu najiyya
19:52.translation: And We called him from the side of the mount at [his] right and brought him near, confiding [to him].

19:53.transliteration: Wawahabna lahu min rahmatinaakhahu haroona nabiyya
19:53.translation: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet.

19:54.transliteration: Wathkur fee alkitabiismaAAeela innahu kana sadiqa alwaAAdi wakanarasoolan nabiyya
19:54.translation: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.

19:55.transliteration: Wakana ya/muru ahlahu bissalatiwazzakati wakana AAinda rabbihi mardiyya
19:55.translation: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakāh and was to his Lord pleasing [i.e., accepted by Him].

19:56.transliteration: Wathkur fee alkitabiidreesa innahu kana siddeeqan nabiyya
19:56.translation: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet.

19:57.translation: And We raised him to a high station.

19:58.transliteration: Ola-ika allatheena anAAama AllahuAAalayhim mina annabiyyeena min thurriyyati adamawamimman hamalna maAAa noohin wamin thurriyyatiibraheema wa-isra-eela wamimman hadayna wajtabaynaitha tutla AAalayhim ayatu arrahmanikharroo sujjadan wabukiyya
19:58.translation: Those were the ones upon whom Allāh bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel [i.e., Jacob], and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.

19:59.transliteration: Fakhalafa min baAAdihim khalfun adaAAooassalata wattabaAAoo ashshahawatifasawfa yalqawna ghayya
19:59.translation: But there came after them successors [i.e., later generations] who neglected prayer and pursued desires; so they are going to meet evil -

19:60.transliteration: Illa man taba waamanawaAAamila salihan faola-ika yadkhuloonaaljannata wala yuthlamoona shay-a
19:60.translation: Except those who repent, believe and do righteousness; for those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged at all.

19:61.transliteration: Jannati AAadnin allatee waAAada arrahmanuAAibadahu bighaybi innahu kana waAAduhuma/tiyya
19:61.translation: [Therein are] gardens of perpetual residence which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen. Indeed, His promise has ever been eminent.

19:62.transliteration: La yasmaAAoona feeha laghwanilla salaman walahum rizquhum feeha bukratanwaAAashiyya
19:62.translation: They will not hear therein any ill speech - only [greetings of] peace - and they will have their provision therein, morning and afternoon.

19:63.transliteration: Tilka aljannatu allatee noorithu min AAibadinaman kana taqiyya
19:63.translation: That is Paradise, which We give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were fearing of Allāh.

19:64.transliteration: Wama natanazzalu illa bi-amrirabbika lahu ma bayna aydeena wama khalfanawama bayna thalika wama kana rabbukanasiyya
19:64.translation: [Gabriel said], "And we [angels] descend not except by the order of your Lord. To Him belongs that before us and that behind us and what is in between. And never is your Lord forgetful -

19:65.transliteration: Rabbu assamawati wal-ardiwama baynahuma faAAbudhu wastabirliAAibadatihi hal taAAlamu lahu samiyya
19:65.translation: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them - so worship Him and have patience for His worship. Do you know of any similarity to Him?"

19:66.transliteration: Wayaqoolu al-insanu a-itha mamittu lasawfa okhraju hayya
19:66.translation: And man [i.e., the disbeliever] says, "When I have died, am I going to be brought forth alive?"

19:67.transliteration: Awa la yathkuru al-insanuanna khalaqnahu min qablu walam yaku shay-a
19:67.translation: Does man not remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?

19:68.transliteration: Fawarabbika lanahshurannahum washshayateenathumma lanuhdirannahum hawla jahannama jithiyya
19:68.translation: So by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils; then We will bring them to be present around Hell upon their knees.

19:69.transliteration: Thumma lananziAAanna min kulli sheeAAatinayyuhum ashaddu AAala arrahmaniAAitiyya
19:69.translation: Then We will surely extract from every sect those of them who were worst against the Most Merciful in insolence.

19:70.transliteration: Thumma lanahnu aAAlamu billatheenahum awla biha siliyya
19:70.translation: Then, surely it is We who are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein.

19:71.transliteration: Wa-in minkum illa wariduhakana AAala rabbika hatman maqdiyya
19:71.translation: And there is none of you except he will come to it. This is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.

19:72.transliteration: Thumma nunajjee allatheena ittaqawwanatharu aththalimeena feehajithiyya
19:72.translation: Then We will save those who feared Allāh and leave the wrongdoers within it, on their knees.

19:73.transliteration: Wa-itha tutla AAalayhim ayatunabayyinatin qala allatheena kafaroo lillatheenaamanoo ayyu alfareeqayni khayrun maqaman waahsanunadiyya
19:73.translation: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Which of [our] two parties is best in position and best in association?"

19:74.transliteration: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin humahsanu athathan wari/ya
19:74.translation: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them who were better in possessions and [outward] appearance?

19:75.transliteration: Qul man kana fee addalalatifalyamdud lahu arrahmanu maddan hattaitha raaw ma yooAAadoona imma alAAathabawa-imma assaAAata fasayaAAlamoona man huwasharrun makanan waadAAafu junda
19:75.translation: Say, "Whoever is in error - let the Most Merciful extend for him an extension [in wealth and time] until, when they see that which they were promised - either punishment [in this world] or the Hour [of resurrection] - they will come to know who is worst in position and weaker in soldiers."

19:76.transliteration: Wayazeedu Allahu allatheenaihtadaw hudan walbaqiyatu assalihatukhayrun AAinda rabbika thawaban wakhayrun maradda
19:76.translation: And Allāh increases those who were guided, in guidance, and the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for recourse.

19:77.transliteration: Afaraayta allathee kafara bi-ayatinawaqala laootayanna malan wawalada
19:77.translation: Then, have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, "I will surely be given wealth and children [in the next life]"?

19:78.transliteration: AttalaAAa alghayba ami ittakhathaAAinda arrahmani AAahda
19:78.translation: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken from the Most Merciful a promise?

19:79.transliteration: Kalla sanaktubu ma yaqooluwanamuddu lahu mina alAAathabi madda
19:79.translation: No! We will record what he says and extend [i.e., increase] for him from the punishment extensively.

19:80.translation: And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.

19:81.transliteration: Wattakhathoo min dooni Allahialihatan liyakoonoo lahum AAizza
19:81.translation: And they have taken besides Allāh [false] deities that they would be for them [a source of] honor.

19:82.translation: No! They [i.e., those "gods"] will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents [on the Day of Judgement].

19:83.transliteration: Alam tara anna arsalna ashshayateenaAAala alkafireena taozzuhum azza
19:83.translation: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them [to evil] with [constant] incitement?

19:84.transliteration: Fala taAAjal AAalayhim innamanaAAuddu lahum AAadda
19:84.translation: So be not impatient over them. We only count out [i.e., allow] to them a [limited] number.

19:85.translation: On the Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation

19:86.translation: And will drive the criminals to Hell in thirst

19:87.transliteration: La yamlikoona ashshafaAAatailla mani ittakhatha AAinda arrahmaniAAahda
19:87.translation: None will have [power of] intercession except he who had taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.

19:88.translation: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken [for Himself] a son."

19:89.translation: You have done an atrocious thing.

19:90.transliteration: Takadu assamawatuyatafattarna minhu watanshaqqu al-ardu watakhirrualjibalu hadda
19:90.translation: The heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation

19:91.translation: That they attribute to the Most Merciful a son.

19:92.translation: And it is not appropriate for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.

19:93.transliteration: In kullu man fee assamawatiwal-ardi illa atee arrahmaniAAabda
19:93.translation: There is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.

19:94.translation: He has enumerated them and counted them a [full] counting.

19:95.translation: And all of them are coming to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.

19:96.transliteration: Inna allatheena amanoowaAAamiloo assalihati sayajAAalu lahumu arrahmanuwudda
19:96.translation: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them affection.

19:97.transliteration: Fa-innama yassarnahu bilisanikalitubashshira bihi almuttaqeena watunthira bihi qawmanludda
19:97.translation: So, [O Muḥammad], We have only made it [i.e., the Qur’ān] easy in your tongue [i.e., the Arabic language] that you may give good tidings thereby to the righteous and warn thereby a hostile people.

19:98.transliteration: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin haltuhissu minhum min ahadin aw tasmaAAu lahum rikza
19:98.translation: And how many have We destroyed before them of generations? Do you perceive of them anyone or hear from them a sound?

20:2.translation: We have not sent down to you the Qur’ān that you be distressed

20:3.translation: But only as a reminder for those who fear [Allāh] -

20:4.translation: A revelation from He who created the earth and highest heavens,

20:5.translation: The Most Merciful [who is] above the Throne established.

20:6.transliteration: Lahu ma fee assamawatiwama fee al-ardi wama baynahuma wamatahta aththara
20:6.translation: To Him belongs what is in the heavens and what is on the earth and what is between them and what is under the soil.

20:7.transliteration: Wa-in tajhar bilqawli fa-innahuyaAAlamu assirra waakhfa
20:7.translation: And if you speak aloud - then indeed, He knows the secret and what is [even] more hidden.

20:8.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illahuwa lahu al-asmao alhusna
20:8.translation: Allāh - there is no deity except Him. To Him belong the best names.

20:9.translation: And has the story of Moses reached you? -

20:10.transliteration: Ith raa naran faqalali-ahlihi omkuthoo innee anastu naran laAAallee ateekumminha biqabasin aw ajidu AAala annarihuda
20:10.translation: When he saw a fire and said to his family, "Stay here; indeed, I have perceived a fire; perhaps I can bring you a torch or find at the fire some guidance."

20:11.translation: And when he came to it, he was called, "O Moses,

20:12.transliteration: Innee ana rabbuka fakhlaAAnaAAlayka innaka bilwadi almuqaddasi tuwa
20:12.translation: Indeed, I am your Lord, so remove your sandals. Indeed, you are in the blessed valley of Ṭuwā.

20:13.translation: And I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed [to you].

20:14.transliteration: Innanee ana Allahu lailaha illa ana faAAbudnee waaqimi assalatalithikree
20:14.translation: Indeed, I am Allāh. There is no deity except Me, so worship Me and establish prayer for My remembrance.

20:15.transliteration: Inna assaAAata atiyatunakadu okhfeeha litujza kullu nafsin bimatasAAa
20:15.translation: Indeed, the Hour is coming - I almost conceal it - so that every soul may be recompensed according to that for which it strives.

20:16.transliteration: Fala yasuddannaka AAanhaman la yu/minu biha wattabaAAa hawahufatarda
20:16.translation: So do not let one avert you from it who does not believe in it and follows his desire, for you [then] would perish.

20:17.translation: And what is that in your right hand, O Moses?"

20:18.transliteration: Qala hiya AAasaya atawakkaoAAalayha waahushshu biha AAala ghanameewaliya feeha maaribu okhra
20:18.translation: He said, "It is my staff; I lean upon it, and I bring down leaves for my sheep and I have therein other uses."

20:20.translation: So he threw it down, and thereupon it was a snake, moving swiftly.

20:21.transliteration: Qala khuthha walatakhaf sanuAAeeduha seerataha al-oola
20:21.translation: [Allāh] said, "Seize it and fear not; We will return it to its former condition.

20:22.transliteration: Wadmum yadaka ila janahikatakhruj baydaa min ghayri soo-in ayatan okhra
20:22.translation: And draw in your hand to your side; it will come out white without disease - another sign,

20:23.translation: That We may show you [some] of Our greater signs.

20:24.translation: Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed [i.e., tyrannized]."

20:25.translation: [Moses] said, "My Lord, expand [i.e., relax] for me my breast [with assurance]

20:26.translation: And ease for me my task

20:27.translation: And untie the knot from my tongue

20:28.translation: That they may understand my speech.

20:29.translation: And appoint for me a minister [i.e., assistant] from my family -

20:32.translation: And let him share my task

20:33.translation: That we may exalt You much

20:35.translation: Indeed, You are of us ever Seeing."

20:36.translation: [Allāh] said, "You have been granted your request, O Moses.

20:37.translation: And We had already conferred favor upon you another time,

20:38.translation: When We inspired to your mother what We inspired,

20:39.transliteration: Ani iqthifeehi fee attabootifaqthifeehi fee alyammi falyulqihi alyammu bissahiliya/khuthhu AAaduwwun lee waAAaduwwun lahu waalqaytuAAalayka mahabbatan minnee walitusnaAAa AAalaAAaynee
20:39.translation: [Saying], 'Cast him into the chest and cast it into the river, and the river will throw it onto the bank; there will take him an enemy to Me and an enemy to him.' And I bestowed upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye [i.e., observation and care].

20:40.transliteration: Ith tamshee okhtuka fataqoolu haladullukum AAala man yakfuluhu farajaAAnaka ilaommika kay taqarra AAaynuha wala tahzanawaqatalta nafsan fanajjaynaka mina alghammi wafatannakafutoonan falabithta sineena fee ahli madyana thumma ji/ta AAalaqadarin ya moosa
20:40.translation: [And We favored you] when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a [severe] trial. And you remained [some] years among the people of Madyan. Then you came [here] at the decreed time, O Moses.

20:41.translation: And I produced you for Myself.

20:42.transliteration: Ithhab anta waakhooka bi-ayateewala taniya fee thikree
20:42.translation: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not slacken in My remembrance.

20:43.translation: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.

20:44.transliteration: Faqoola lahu qawlan layyinanlaAAallahu yatathakkaru aw yakhsha
20:44.translation: And speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear [Allāh]."

20:45.transliteration: Qala rabbana innananakhafu an yafruta AAalayna aw an yatgha
20:45.translation: They said, "Our Lord, indeed we are afraid that he will hasten [punishment] against us or that he will transgress."

20:46.transliteration: Qala la takhafainnanee maAAakuma asmaAAu waara
20:46.translation: [Allāh] said, "Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.

20:47.transliteration: Fa/tiyahu faqoola innarasoola rabbika faarsil maAAana banee isra-eelawala tuAAaththibhum qad ji/naka bi-ayatinmin rabbika wassalamu AAala mani ittabaAAaalhuda
20:47.translation: So go to him and say, 'Indeed, we are messengers of your Lord, so send with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon he who follows the guidance.

20:48.transliteration: Inna qad oohiya ilaynaanna alAAathaba AAala man kaththaba watawalla
20:48.translation: Indeed, it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away.'"

20:49.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "So who is the Lord of you two, O Moses?"

20:50.transliteration: Qala rabbuna allatheeaAAta kulla shay-in khalqahu thumma hada
20:50.translation: He said, "Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided [it]."

20:51.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "Then what is the case of the former generations?"

20:52.transliteration: Qala AAilmuha AAinda rabbeefee kitabin la yadillu rabbee walayansa
20:52.translation: [Moses] said, "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets."

20:53.transliteration: Allathee jaAAala lakumu al-ardamahdan wasalaka lakum feeha subulan waanzala mina assama-imaan faakhrajna bihi azwajan min nabatinshatta
20:53.translation: [It is He] who has made for you the earth as a bed [spread out] and inserted therein for you roadways and sent down from the sky, rain and produced thereby categories of various plants.

20:54.transliteration: Kuloo warAAaw anAAamakum innafee thalika laayatin li-olee annuha
20:54.translation: Eat [therefrom] and pasture your livestock. Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.

20:55.transliteration: Minha khalaqnakum wafeehanuAAeedukum waminha nukhrijukum taratan okhra
20:55.translation: From it [i.e., the earth] We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will extract you another time.

20:56.translation: And We certainly showed him [i.e., Pharaoh] Our signs - all of them - but he denied and refused.

20:57.transliteration: Qala aji/tana litukhrijanamin ardina bisihrika ya moosa
20:57.translation: He said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?

20:58.transliteration: Falana/tiyannaka bisihrin mithlihi fajAAalbaynana wabaynaka mawAAidan la nukhlifuhu nahnuwala anta makanan suwa
20:58.translation: Then we will surely bring you magic like it, so make between us and you an appointment, which we will not fail to keep and neither will you, in a place assigned."

20:59.transliteration: Qala mawAAidukum yawmu azzeenatiwaan yuhshara annasu duha
20:59.translation: [Moses] said, "Your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid-morning."

20:60.translation: So Pharaoh went away, put together his plan, and then came [to Moses].

20:61.transliteration: Qala lahum moosa waylakum lataftaroo AAala Allahi kathiban fayushitakumbiAAathabin waqad khaba mani iftara
20:61.translation: Moses said to them [i.e., the magicians summoned by Pharaoh], "Woe to you! Do not invent a lie against Allāh or He will exterminate you with a punishment; and he has failed who invents [such falsehood]."

20:62.translation: So they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation.

20:63.transliteration: Qaloo in hathani lasahiraniyureedani an yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihimawayathhaba bitareeqatikumu almuthla
20:63.translation: They said, "Indeed, these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way [i.e., religion or tradition].

20:64.transliteration: FaajmiAAoo kaydakum thumma i/too saffanwaqad aflaha alyawma mani istaAAla
20:64.translation: So resolve upon your plan and then come [forward] in line. And he has succeeded today who overcomes."

20:65.transliteration: Qaloo ya moosa immaan tulqiya wa-imma an nakoona awwala man alqa
20:65.translation: They said, "O Moses, either you throw or we will be the first to throw."

20:66.transliteration: Qala bal alqoo fa-itha hibaluhumwaAAisiyyuhum yukhayyalu ilayhi min sihrihim annahatasAAa
20:66.translation: He said, "Rather, you throw." And suddenly their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their magic that they were moving [like snakes].

20:67.translation: And he sensed within himself apprehension, did Moses.

20:68.translation: We [i.e., Allāh] said, "Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior.

20:69.transliteration: Waalqi ma fee yameenika talqaf masanaAAoo innama sanaAAoo kaydu sahirinwala yuflihu assahiru haythuata
20:69.translation: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted. What they have crafted is but the trick of a magician, and the magician will not succeed wherever he is."

20:70.transliteration: Faolqiya assaharatu sujjadanqaloo amanna birabbi haroona wamoosa
20:70.translation: So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, "We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses."

20:71.transliteration: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athanalakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu assihrafalaoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakum min khilafinwalaosallibannakum fee juthooAAi annakhliwalataAAlamunna ayyuna ashaddu AAathaban waabqa
20:71.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed him [i.e., Moses] before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in [giving] punishment and more enduring."

20:72.transliteration: Qaloo lan nu/thiraka AAala majaana mina albayyinati wallatheefatarana faqdi ma anta qadininnama taqdee hathihi alhayataaddunya
20:72.translation: They said, "Never will we prefer you over what has come to us of clear proofs and [over] He who created us. So decree whatever you are to decree. You can only decree for this worldly life.

20:73.transliteration: Inna amanna birabbinaliyaghfira lana khatayana wamaakrahtana AAalayhi mina assihri wallahukhayrun waabqa
20:73.translation: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and what you compelled us [to do] of magic. And Allāh is better and more enduring."

20:74.transliteration: Innahu man ya/ti rabbahu mujriman fa-innalahu jahannama la yamootu feeha wala yahya
20:74.translation: Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal - indeed, for him is Hell; he will neither die therein nor live.

20:75.transliteration: Waman ya/tihi mu/minan qad AAamila assalihatifaola-ika lahumu addarajatu alAAula
20:75.translation: But whoever comes to Him as a believer having done righteous deeds - for those will be the highest degrees [in position]:

20:76.transliteration: Jannatu AAadnin tajree min tahtihaal-anharu khalideena feeha wathalikajazao man tazakka
20:76.translation: Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. And that is the reward of one who purifies himself.

20:77.transliteration: Walaqad awhayna ilamoosa an asri biAAibadee fadriblahum tareeqan fee albahri yabasan la takhafudarakan wala takhsha
20:77.translation: And We had inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken [by Pharaoh] nor be afraid [of drowning]."

20:78.transliteration: FaatbaAAahum firAAawnu bijunoodihifaghashiyahum mina alyammi ma ghashiyahum
20:78.translation: So Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers, and there covered them from the sea that which covered them,

20:79.translation: And Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide [them].

20:80.transliteration: Ya banee isra-eela qad anjaynakummin AAaduwwikum wawaAAadnakum janiba attoorial-aymana wanazzalna AAalaykumu almanna wassalwa
20:80.translation: O Children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy, and We made an appointment with you at the right side of the mount, and We sent down to you manna and quails,

20:81.transliteration: Kuloo min tayyibati marazaqnakum wala tatghaw feehi fayahillaAAalaykum ghadabee waman yahlil AAalayhi ghadabeefaqad hawa
20:81.translation: [Saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you and do not transgress [or oppress others] therein, lest My anger should descend upon you. And he upon whom My anger descends has certainly fallen [i.e., perished]."

20:82.transliteration: Wa-innee laghaffarun liman tabawaamana waAAamila salihan thumma ihtada
20:82.translation: But indeed, I am the Perpetual Forgiver of whoever repents and believes and does righteousness and then continues in guidance.

20:83.translation: [Allāh said], "And what made you hasten from your people, O Moses?"

20:84.transliteration: Qala hum ola-i AAalaatharee waAAajiltu ilayka rabbi litarda
20:84.translation: He said, "They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You be pleased."

20:85.transliteration: Qala fa-inna qad fatannaqawmaka min baAAdika waadallahumu assamiriy
20:85.translation: [Allāh] said, "But indeed, We have tried your people after you [departed], and the Sāmirī has led them astray."

20:86.transliteration: FarajaAAa moosa ila qawmihighadbana asifan qala ya qawmi alamyaAAidkum rabbukum waAAdan hasanan afatalaAAalaykumu alAAahdu am aradtum an yahilla AAalaykum ghadabunmin rabbikum faakhlaftum mawAAidee
20:86.translation: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said, "O my people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Then, was the time [of its fulfillment] too long for you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord descend upon you, so you broke your promise [of obedience] to me?"

20:87.transliteration: Qaloo ma akhlafnamawAAidaka bimalkina walakinna hummilnaawzaran min zeenati alqawmi faqathafnahafakathalika alqa assamiriy
20:87.translation: They said, "We did not break our promise to you by our will, but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people [of Pharaoh], so we threw them [into the fire], and thus did the Sāmirī throw."

20:88.transliteration: Faakhraja lahum AAijlan jasadan lahu khuwarunfaqaloo hatha ilahukum wa-ilahu moosafanasiy
20:88.translation: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot."

20:89.transliteration: Afala yarawna alla yarjiAAuilayhim qawlan wala yamliku lahum darran walanafAAa
20:89.translation: Did they not see that it could not return to them any speech [i.e., response] and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit?

20:90.transliteration: Walaqad qala lahum haroonu minqablu ya qawmi innama futintum bihi wa-innarabbakumu arrahmanu fattabiAAooneewaateeAAoo amree
20:90.translation: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order."

20:91.transliteration: Qaloo lan nabraha AAalayhi AAakifeenahatta yarjiAAa ilayna moosa
20:91.translation: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to it [i.e., the calf] until Moses returns to us."

20:92.transliteration: Qala ya haroonu mamanaAAaka ith raaytahum dalloo
20:92.translation: [Moses] said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

20:93.translation: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order?"

20:94.transliteration: Qala yabnaomma la ta/khuthbilihyatee wala bira/see innee khasheetu an taqoolafarraqta bayna banee isra-eela walam tarqub qawlee
20:94.translation: [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word.'"

20:95.translation: [Moses] said, "And what is your case, O Sāmirī?"

20:96.transliteration: Qala basurtu bima lamyabsuroo bihi faqabadtu qabdatan min athariarrasooli fanabathtuha wakathalikasawwalat lee nafsee
20:96.translation: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me."

20:97.transliteration: Qala fathhab fa-innalaka fee alhayati an taqoola la misasawa-inna laka mawAAidan lan tukhlafahu wanthurila ilahika allathee thaltaAAalayhi AAakifan lanuharriqannahu thummalanansifannahu fee alyammi nasfa
20:97.translation: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it [i.e., its ashes] into the sea with a blast.

20:98.transliteration: Innama ilahukumu Allahuallathee la ilaha illa huwa wasiAAakulla shay-in AAilma
20:98.translation: Your god is only Allāh, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge."

20:99.transliteration: Kathalika naqussu AAalayka minanba-i ma qad sabaqa waqad ataynakamin ladunna thikra
20:99.translation: Thus, [O Muḥammad], We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us a message [i.e., the Qur’ān].

20:100.transliteration: Man aAArada AAanhu fa-innahu yahmiluyawma alqiyamati wizra
20:100.translation: Whoever turns away from it - then indeed, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden [i.e., great sin],

20:101.transliteration: Khalideena feehi wasaa lahumyawma alqiyamati himla
20:101.translation: [Abiding] eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load -

20:102.transliteration: Yawma yunfakhu fee assooriwanahshuru almujrimeena yawma-ithin zurqa
20:102.translation: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed.

20:103.translation: They will murmur among themselves, "You remained not but ten [days in the world]."

20:104.transliteration: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yaqooloonaith yaqoolu amthaluhum tareeqatan in labithtum illayawma
20:104.translation: We are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner [i.e., wisdom or speech] will say, "You remained not but one day."

20:105.transliteration: Wayas-aloonaka AAani aljibali faqulyansifuha rabbee nasfa
20:105.translation: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, "My Lord will blow them away with a blast.

20:106.translation: And He will leave it [i.e., the earth] a level plain;

20:107.translation: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation."

20:108.transliteration: Yawma-ithin yattabiAAoona addaAAiyala AAiwaja lahu wakhashaAAati al-aswatu lirrahmanifala tasmaAAu illa hamsa
20:108.translation: That Day, they [i.e., everyone] will follow [the call of] the Caller [with] no deviation therefrom, and [all] voices will be stilled before the Most Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper [of footsteps].

20:109.transliteration: Yawma-ithin la tanfaAAu ashshafaAAatuilla man athina lahu arrahmanuwaradiya lahu qawla
20:109.translation: That Day, no intercession will benefit except [that of] one to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word.

20:110.transliteration: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wamakhalfahum wala yuheetoona bihi AAilma
20:110.translation: He [i.e., Allāh] knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, but they do not encompass it [i.e., what He knows] in knowledge.

20:111.transliteration: WaAAanati alwujoohu lilhayyialqayyoomi waqad khaba man hamala thulma
20:111.translation: And [all] faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. And he will have failed who carries injustice.

20:112.transliteration: Waman yaAAmal mina assalihatiwahuwa mu/minun fala yakhafu thulmanwala hadma
20:112.translation: But he who does of righteous deeds while he is a believer - he will neither fear injustice nor deprivation.

20:113.transliteration: Wakathalika anzalnahu qur-ananAAarabiyyan wasarrafna feehi mina alwaAAeedilaAAallahum yattaqoona aw yuhdithu lahum thikra
20:113.translation: And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’ān and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid [sin] or it would cause them remembrance.

20:114.transliteration: FataAAala Allahualmaliku alhaqqu wala taAAjal bilqur-animin qabli an yuqda ilayka wahyuhu waqul rabbizidnee AAilma
20:114.translation: So high [above all] is Allāh, the Sovereign, the Truth. And, [O Muḥammad], do not hasten with [recitation of] the Qur’ān before its revelation is completed to you, and say, "My Lord, increase me in knowledge."

20:115.transliteration: Walaqad AAahidna ila adamamin qablu fanasiya walam najid lahu AAazma
20:115.translation: And We had already taken a promise from Adam before, but he forgot; and We found not in him determination.

20:116.transliteration: Wa-ith qulna lilmala-ikatiosjudoo li-adama fasajadoo illa ibleesa aba
20:116.translation: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except Iblees; he refused.

20:117.transliteration: Faqulna ya adamu innahatha AAaduwwun laka walizawjika falayukhrijannakuma mina aljannati fatashqa
20:117.translation: So We said, "O Adam, indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Then let him not remove you from Paradise so you would suffer.

20:118.transliteration: Inna laka alla tajooAAa feehawala taAAra
20:118.translation: Indeed, it is [promised] for you not to be hungry therein or be unclothed.

20:119.translation: And indeed, you will not be thirsty therein or be hot from the sun."

20:120.transliteration: Fawaswasa ilayhi ashshaytanuqala ya adamu hal adulluka AAalashajarati alkhuldi wamulkin la yabla
20:120.translation: Then Satan whispered to him; he said, "O Adam, shall I direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate?"

20:121.transliteration: Faakala minha fabadat lahumasaw-atuhuma watafiqa yakhsifaniAAalayhima min waraqi aljannati waAAasa adamurabbahu faghawa
20:121.translation: And they [i.e., Adam and his wife] ate of it, and their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten over themselves from the leaves of Paradise. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.

20:122.translation: Then his Lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided [him].

20:123.transliteration: Qala ihbita minhajameeAAan baAAdukum libaAAdin AAaduwwun fa-immaya/tiyannakum minnee hudan famani ittabaAAa hudaya falayadillu wala yashqa
20:123.translation: [Allāh] said, "Descend from it [i.e., Paradise] - all, [your descendants] being enemies to one another. And if there should come to you guidance from Me - then whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray [in the world] nor suffer [in the Hereafter].

20:124.transliteration: Waman aAArada AAan thikreefa-inna lahu maAAeeshatan danka wanahshuruhuyawma alqiyamati aAAma
20:124.translation: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed [i.e., difficult] life, and We will gather [i.e., raise] him on the Day of Resurrection blind."

20:125.transliteration: Qala rabbi lima hashartaneeaAAma waqad kuntu baseera
20:125.translation: He will say, "My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?"

20:126.transliteration: Qala kathalika atatka ayatunafanaseetaha wakathalika alyawma tunsa
20:126.translation: [Allāh] will say, "Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot [i.e., disregarded] them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten."

20:127.transliteration: Wakathalika najzee man asrafa walamyu/min bi-ayati rabbihi walaAAathabu al-akhiratiashaddu waabqa
20:127.translation: And thus do We recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more enduring.

20:128.transliteration: Afalam yahdi lahum kam ahlaknaqablahum mina alqurooni yamshoona fee masakinihim inna feethalika laayatin li-olee annuha
20:128.translation: Then, has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.

20:129.transliteration: Walawla kalimatun sabaqat minrabbika lakana lizaman waajalun musamma
20:129.translation: And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it [i.e., punishment] would have been an obligation [due immediately], and [if not for] a specified term [decreed].

20:130.transliteration: Fasbir AAala mayaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAiashshamsi waqabla ghuroobiha wamin ana-iallayli fasabbih waatrafa annaharilaAAallaka tarda
20:130.translation: So be patient over what they say and exalt [Allāh] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during periods of the night [exalt Him] and at the ends of the day, that you may be satisfied.

20:131.transliteration: Wala tamuddanna AAaynayka ilama mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum zahrata alhayatiaddunya linaftinahum feehi warizqu rabbika khayrunwaabqa
