24:6.transliteration: Wallatheena yarmoona azwajahumwalam yakun lahum shuhadao illa anfusuhum fashahadatuahadihim arbaAAu shahadatin billahiinnahu lamina assadiqeen
24:6.translation: And those who accuse their wives [of adultery] and have no witnesses except themselves - then the witness of one of them [shall be] four testimonies [swearing] by Allāh that indeed, he is of the truthful.

24:7.transliteration: Walkhamisatu anna laAAnata AllahiAAalayhi in kana mina alkathibeen
24:7.translation: And the fifth [oath will be] that the curse of Allāh be upon him if he should be of the liars.

24:8.transliteration: Wayadrao AAanha alAAathaba antashhada arbaAAa shahadatin billahiinnahu lamina alkathibeen
24:8.translation: But it will prevent punishment from her if she gives four testimonies [swearing] by Allāh that indeed, he is of the liars.

24:9.transliteration: Walkhamisata anna ghadabaAllahi AAalayha in kana mina assadiqeen
24:9.translation: And the fifth [oath will be] that the wrath of Allāh be upon her if he was of the truthful.

24:10.transliteration: Walawla fadlu AllahiAAalaykum warahmatuhu waanna Allaha tawwabunhakeem
24:10.translation: And if not for the favor of Allāh upon you and His mercy... and because Allāh is Accepting of Repentance and Wise.

24:11.transliteration: Inna allatheena jaoo bil-ifkiAAusbatun minkum la tahsaboohu sharran lakumbal huwa khayrun lakum likulli imri-in minhum ma iktasabamina al-ithmi wallathee tawalla kibrahuminhum lahu AAathabun AAatheem
24:11.translation: Indeed, those who came with falsehood are a group among you. Do not think it bad for you; rather, it is good for you. For every person among them is what [punishment] he has earned from the sin, and he who took upon himself the greater portion thereof - for him is a great punishment [i.e., Hellfire].

24:12.transliteration: Lawla ith samiAAtumoohu thannaalmu/minoona walmu/minatu bi-anfusihim khayran waqaloohatha ifkun mubeen
24:12.translation: Why, when you heard it, did not the believing men and believing women think good of themselves [i.e., one another] and say, "This is an obvious falsehood"?

24:13.transliteration: Lawla jaoo AAalayhibi-arbaAAati shuhadaa fa-ith lam ya/too bishshuhada-ifaola-ika AAinda Allahi humu alkathiboon
24:13.translation: Why did they [who slandered] not produce for it four witnesses? And when they do not produce the witnesses, then it is they, in the sight of Allāh, who are the liars.

24:14.transliteration: Walawla fadlu AllahiAAalaykum warahmatuhu fee addunya wal-akhiratilamassakum fee ma afadtum feehi AAathabunAAatheem
24:14.translation: And if it had not been for the favor of Allāh upon you and His mercy in this world and the Hereafter, you would have been touched for that [lie] in which you were involved by a great punishment

24:15.transliteration: Ith talaqqawnahu bi-alsinatikumwataqooloona bi-afwahikum ma laysa lakum bihiAAilmun watahsaboonahu hayyinan wahuwa AAinda AllahiAAatheem
24:15.translation: When you received it with your tongues and said with your mouths that of which you had no knowledge and thought it was insignificant while it was, in the sight of Allāh, tremendous.

24:16.transliteration: Walawla ith samiAAtumoohuqultum ma yakoonu lana an natakallama bihathasubhanaka hatha buhtanun AAatheem
24:16.translation: And why, when you heard it, did you not say, "It is not for us to speak of this. Exalted are You, [O Allāh]; this is a great slander"?

24:17.transliteration: YaAAithukumu Allahu antaAAoodoo limithlihi abadan in kuntum mu/mineen
24:17.translation: Allāh warns you against returning to the likes of this [conduct], ever, if you should be believers.

24:18.transliteration: Wayubayyinu Allahu lakumu al-ayatiwallahu AAaleemun hakeem
24:18.translation: And Allāh makes clear to you the verses [i.e., His rulings], and Allāh is Knowing and Wise.

24:19.transliteration: Inna allatheena yuhibboona antasheeAAa alfahishatu fee allatheena amanoolahum AAathabun aleemun fee addunya wal-akhiratiwallahu yaAAlamu waantum la taAAlamoon
24:19.translation: Indeed, those who like that immorality should be spread [or publicized] among those who have believed will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter. And Allāh knows and you do not know.

24:20.transliteration: Walawla fadlu AllahiAAalaykum warahmatuhu waanna Allaha raoofun raheem
24:20.translation: And if it had not been for the favor of Allāh upon you and His mercy... and because Allāh is Kind and Merciful.

24:21.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoola tattabiAAoo khutuwati ashshaytaniwaman yattabiAA khutuwati ashshaytanifa-innahu ya/muru bilfahsha-i walmunkariwalawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhuma zaka minkum min ahadin abadan walakinnaAllaha yuzakkee man yashao wallahusameeAAun AAaleem
24:21.translation: O you who have believed, do not follow the footsteps of Satan. And whoever follows the footsteps of Satan - indeed, he enjoins immorality and wrongdoing. And if not for the favor of Allāh upon you and His mercy, not one of you would have been pure, ever, but Allāh purifies whom He wills, and Allāh is Hearing and Knowing.

24:22.transliteration: Wala ya/tali oloo alfadliminkum wassaAAati an yu/too olee alqurba walmasakeenawalmuhajireena fee sabeeli AllahiwalyaAAfoo walyasfahoo ala tuhibboonaan yaghfira Allahu lakum wallahu ghafoorunraheem
24:22.translation: And let not those of virtue among you and wealth swear not to give [aid] to their relatives and the needy and the emigrants for the cause of Allāh, and let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allāh should forgive you? And Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

24:23.transliteration: Inna allatheena yarmoona almuhsanatialghafilati almu/minati luAAinoo fee addunyawal-akhirati walahum AAathabun AAatheem
24:23.translation: Indeed, those who [falsely] accuse chaste, unaware and believing women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter; and they will have a great punishment

24:24.transliteration: Yawma tashhadu AAalayhim alsinatuhumwaaydeehim waarjuluhum bima kanoo yaAAmaloon
24:24.translation: On a Day when their tongues, their hands and their feet will bear witness against them as to what they used to do.

24:25.transliteration: Yawma-ithin yuwaffeehimu Allahudeenahumu alhaqqa wayaAAlamoona anna Allaha huwa alhaqqualmubeen
24:25.translation: That Day, Allāh will pay them in full their true [i.e., deserved] recompense, and they will know that it is Allāh who is the manifest Truth [i.e., perfect in justice].

24:26.transliteration: Alkhabeethatu lilkhabeetheena walkhabeethoonalilkhabeethati wattayyibatu littayyibeenawattayyiboona littayyibatiola-ika mubarraoona mimma yaqooloona lahummaghfiratun warizqun kareem
24:26.translation: Evil words are for evil men, and evil men are [subjected] to evil words. And good words are for good men, and good men are [an object] of good words. Those [good people] are declared innocent of what they [i.e., slanderers] say. For them is forgiveness and noble provision.

24:27.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoola tadkhuloo buyootan ghayra buyootikum hattatasta/nisoo watusallimoo AAala ahliha thalikumkhayrun lakum laAAallakum tathakkaroon
24:27.translation: O you who have believed, do not enter houses other than your own houses until you ascertain welcome and greet their inhabitants. That is best for you; perhaps you will be reminded [i.e., advised].

24:28.transliteration: Fa-in lam tajidoo feeha ahadanfala tadkhulooha hatta yu/thanalakum wa-in qeela lakumu irjiAAoo farjiAAoo huwa azkalakum wallahu bima taAAmaloona AAaleem
24:28.translation: And if you do not find anyone therein, do not enter them until permission has been given you. And if it is said to you, "Go back," then go back; it is purer for you. And Allāh is Knowing of what you do.

24:29.transliteration: Laysa AAalaykum junahun an tadkhuloobuyootan ghayra maskoonatin feeha mataAAun lakum wallahuyaAAlamu ma tubdoona wama taktumoon
24:29.translation: There is no blame upon you for entering houses not inhabited in which there is convenience for you. And Allāh knows what you reveal and what you conceal.

24:30.transliteration: Qul lilmu/mineena yaghuddoo min absarihumwayahfathoo furoojahum thalika azkalahum inna Allaha khabeerun bima yasnaAAoon
24:30.translation: Tell the believing men to reduce [some] of their vision and guard their private parts. That is purer for them. Indeed, Allāh is [fully] Aware of what they do.

24:31.transliteration: Waqul lilmu/minati yaghdudnamin absarihinna wayahfathnafuroojahunna wala yubdeena zeenatahunna illa mathahara minha walyadribnabikhumurihinna AAala juyoobihinna wala yubdeenazeenatahunna illa libuAAoolatihinna aw aba-ihinnaaw aba-i buAAoolatihinna aw abna-ihinna awabna-i buAAoolatihinna aw ikhwanihinna aw baneeikhwanihinna aw banee akhawatihinna aw nisa-ihinnaaw ma malakat aymanuhunna awi attabiAAeenaghayri olee al-irbati mina arrijali awi attifliallatheena lam yathharoo AAala AAawratiannisa-i wala yadribnabi-arjulihinna liyuAAlama ma yukhfeena min zeenatihinnawatooboo ila Allahi jameeAAan ayyuhaalmu/minoona laAAallakum tuflihoon
24:31.translation: And tell the believing women to reduce [some] of their vision and guard their private parts and not expose their adornment except that which [necessarily] appears thereof and to wrap [a portion of] their headcovers over their chests and not expose their adornment [i.e., beauty] except to their husbands, their fathers, their husbands' fathers, their sons, their husbands' sons, their brothers, their brothers' sons, their sisters' sons, their women, that which their right hands possess [i.e., slaves], or those male attendants having no physical desire, or children who are not yet aware of the private aspects of women. And let them not stamp their feet to make known what they conceal of their adornment. And turn to Allāh in repentance, all of you, O believers, that you might succeed.

24:32.transliteration: Waankihoo al-ayamaminkum wassaliheena min AAibadikumwa-ima-ikum in yakoonoo fuqaraa yughnihimu Allahumin fadlihi wallahu wasiAAun AAaleem
24:32.translation: And marry the unmarried among you and the righteous among your male slaves and female slaves. If they should be poor, Allāh will enrich them from His bounty, and Allāh is all-Encompassing and Knowing.

24:33.transliteration: WalyastaAAfifi allatheena layajidoona nikahan hatta yughniyahumu Allahumin fadlihi wallatheena yabtaghoona alkitabamimma malakat aymanukum fakatiboohum inAAalimtum feehim khayran waatoohum min mali Allahiallathee atakum wala tukrihoo fatayatikumAAala albigha-i in aradna tahassunanlitabtaghoo AAarada alhayati addunyawaman yukrihhunna fa-inna Allaha min baAAdi ikrahihinnaghafoorun raheem
24:33.translation: But let them who find not [the means for] marriage abstain [from sexual relations] until Allāh enriches them from His bounty. And those who seek a contract [for eventual emancipation] from among whom your right hands possess - then make a contract with them if you know there is within them goodness and give them from the wealth of Allāh which He has given you. And do not compel your slave girls to prostitution, if they desire chastity, to seek [thereby] the temporary interests of worldly life. And if someone should compel them, then indeed, Allāh is [to them], after their compulsion, Forgiving and Merciful.

24:34.transliteration: Walaqad anzalna ilaykum ayatinmubayyinatin wamathalan mina allatheena khalaw minqablikum wamawAAithatan lilmuttaqeen
24:34.translation: And We have certainly sent down to you distinct verses and examples from those who passed on before you and an admonition for those who fear Allāh.

24:35.transliteration: Allahu nooru assamawatiwal-ardi mathalu noorihi kamishkatin feehamisbahun almisbahu fee zujajatinazzujajatu kaannaha kawkabun durriyyunyooqadu min shajaratin mubarakatin zaytoonatin lasharqiyyatin wala gharbiyyatin yakadu zaytuhayudee-o walaw lam tamsas-hu narun noorun AAalanoorin yahdee Allahu linoorihi man yashao wayadribuAllahu al-amthala linnasi wallahubikulli shay-in AAaleem
24:35.translation: Allāh is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The example of His light is like a niche within which is a lamp; the lamp is within glass, the glass as if it were a pearly [white] star lit from [the oil of] a blessed olive tree, neither of the east nor of the west, whose oil would almost glow even if untouched by fire. Light upon light. Allāh guides to His light whom He wills. And Allāh presents examples for the people, and Allāh is Knowing of all things.

24:36.transliteration: Fee buyootin athina Allahu anturfaAAa wayuthkara feeha ismuhu yusabbihulahu feeha bilghuduwwi wal-asal
24:36.translation: [Such niches are] in houses [i.e., mosques] which Allāh has ordered to be raised and that His name be mentioned [i.e., praised] therein; exalting Him within them in the morning and the evenings

24:37.transliteration: Rijalun la tulheehim tijaratunwala bayAAun AAan thikri Allahi wa-iqamiassalati wa-eeta-i azzakatiyakhafoona yawman tataqallabu feehi alquloobu wal-absar
24:37.translation: [Are] men whom neither commerce nor sale distracts from the remembrance of Allāh and performance of prayer and giving of zakāh. They fear a Day in which the hearts and eyes will [fearfully] turn about -

24:38.transliteration: Liyajziyahumu Allahu ahsana maAAamiloo wayazeedahum min fadlihi wallahuyarzuqu man yashao bighayri hisab
24:38.translation: That Allāh may reward them [according to] the best of what they did and increase them from His bounty. And Allāh gives provision to whom He wills without account [i.e., limit].

24:39.transliteration: Wallatheena kafaroo aAAmaluhumkasarabin biqeeAAatin yahsabuhu aththam-anumaan hatta itha jaahu lamyajidhu shay-an wawajada Allaha AAindahu fawaffahu hisabahuwallahu sareeAAu alhisab
24:39.translation: But those who disbelieved - their deeds are like a mirage in a lowland which a thirsty one thinks is water until, when he comes to it, he finds it is nothing but finds Allāh before him, and He will pay him in full his due; and Allāh is swift in account.

24:40.transliteration: Aw kathulumatin fee bahrinlujjiyyin yaghshahu mawjun min fawqihi mawjun min fawqihisahabun thulumatun baAAduhafawqa baAAdin itha akhraja yadahu lam yakad yarahawaman lam yajAAali Allahu lahu nooran fama lahu minnoor
24:40.translation: Or [they are] like darknesses within an unfathomable sea which is covered by waves, upon which are waves, over which are clouds - darknesses, some of them upon others. When one puts out his hand [therein], he can hardly see it. And he to whom Allāh has not granted light - for him there is no light.

24:41.transliteration: Alam tara anna Allaha yusabbihulahu man fee assamawati wal-ardiwattayru saffatin kullun qadAAalima salatahu watasbeehahu wallahuAAaleemun bima yafAAaloon
24:41.translation: Do you not see that Allāh is exalted by whomever is within the heavens and the earth and [by] the birds with wings spread [in flight]? Each [of them] has known his [means of] prayer and exalting [Him], and Allāh is Knowing of what they do.

24:42.transliteration: Walillahi mulku assamawatiwal-ardi wa-ila Allahi almaseer
24:42.translation: And to Allāh belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Allāh is the destination.

24:43.transliteration: Alam tara anna Allaha yuzjee sahabanthumma yu-allifu baynahu thumma yajAAaluhu rukaman fataraalwadqa yakhruju min khilalihi wayunazzilu mina assama-imin jibalin feeha min baradin fayuseebu bihiman yashao wayasrifuhu AAan man yashao yakadusana barqihi yathhabu bil-absar
24:43.translation: Do you not see that Allāh drives clouds? Then He brings them together; then He makes them into a mass, and you see the rain emerge from within it. And He sends down from the sky, mountains [of clouds] within which is hail, and He strikes with it whom He wills and averts it from whom He wills. The flash of its lightning almost takes away the eyesight.

24:44.transliteration: Yuqallibu Allahu allayla wannaharainna fee thalika laAAibratan li-olee al-absar
24:44.translation: Allāh alternates the night and the day. Indeed in that is a lesson for those who have vision.

24:45.transliteration: Wallahu khalaqa kulla dabbatinmin ma-in faminhum man yamshee AAala batnihiwaminhum man yamshee AAala rijlayni waminhum man yamsheeAAala arbaAAin yakhluqu Allahu ma yashaoinna Allaha AAala kulli shay-in qadeer
24:45.translation: Allāh has created every [living] creature from water. And of them are those that move on their bellies, and of them are those that walk on two legs, and of them are those that walk on four. Allāh creates what He wills. Indeed, Allāh is over all things competent.

24:46.transliteration: Laqad anzalna ayatinmubayyinatin wallahu yahdee man yashaoila siratin mustaqeem
24:46.translation: We have certainly sent down distinct verses. And Allāh guides whom He wills to a straight path.

24:47.transliteration: Wayaqooloona amanna billahiwabirrasooli waataAAna thumma yatawallafareequn minhum min baAAdi thalika wama ola-ikabilmu/mineen
24:47.translation: But they [i.e., the hypocrites] say, "We have believed in Allāh and in the Messenger, and we obey"; then a party of them turns away after that. And those are not believers.

24:48.transliteration: Wa-itha duAAoo ila Allahiwarasoolihi liyahkuma baynahum itha fareequn minhummuAAridoon
24:48.translation: And when they are called to [the words of] Allāh and His Messenger to judge between them, at once a party of them turns aside [in refusal].

24:49.transliteration: Wa-in yakun lahumu alhaqqu ya/tooilayhi muthAAineen
24:49.translation: But if the right is theirs, they come to him in prompt obedience.

24:50.transliteration: Afee quloobihim maradun ami irtabooam yakhafoona an yaheefa Allahu AAalayhimwarasooluhu bal ola-ika humu aththalimoon
24:50.translation: Is there disease in their hearts? Or have they doubted? Or do they fear that Allāh will be unjust to them, or His Messenger? Rather, it is they who are the wrongdoers [i.e., the unjust].

24:51.transliteration: Innama kana qawla almu/mineenaitha duAAoo ila Allahi warasoolihi liyahkumabaynahum an yaqooloo samiAAna waataAAna waola-ikahumu almuflihoon
24:51.translation: The only statement of the [true] believers when they are called to Allāh and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say, "We hear and we obey." And those are the successful.

24:52.transliteration: Waman yutiAAi Allahawarasoolahu wayakhsha Allaha wayattaqhi faola-ikahumu alfa-izoon
24:52.translation: And whoever obeys Allāh and His Messenger and fears Allāh and is conscious of Him - it is those who are the attainers.

24:53.transliteration: Waaqsamoo billahi jahda aymanihimla-in amartahum layakhrujunna qul la tuqsimoo taAAatunmaAAroofatun inna Allaha khabeerun bima taAAmaloon
24:53.translation: And they swear by Allāh their strongest oaths that if you ordered them, they would go forth [in Allāh's cause]. Say, "Do not swear. [Such] obedience is known. Indeed, Allāh is [fully] Aware of that which you do."

24:54.transliteration: Qul ateeAAoo Allaha waateeAAooarrasoola fa-in tawallaw fa-innama AAalayhi mahummila waAAalaykum ma hummiltum wa-in tuteeAAoohutahtadoo wama AAala arrasooli illaalbalaghu almubeen
24:54.translation: Say, "Obey Allāh and obey the Messenger; but if you turn away - then upon him is only that [duty] with which he has been charged, and upon you is that with which you have been charged. And if you obey him, you will be [rightly] guided. And there is not upon the Messenger except the [responsibility for] clear notification."

24:55.transliteration: WaAAada Allahu allatheena amanoominkum waAAamiloo assalihatilayastakhlifannahum fee al-ardi kama istakhlafaallatheena min qablihim walayumakkinanna lahum deenahumuallathee irtada lahum walayubaddilannahum minbaAAdi khawfihim amnan yaAAbudoonanee la yushrikoona beeshay-an waman kafara baAAda thalika faola-ika humualfasiqoon
24:55.translation: Allāh has promised those who have believed among you and done righteous deeds that He will surely grant them succession [to authority] upon the earth just as He granted it to those before them and that He will surely establish for them [therein] their religion which He has preferred for them and that He will surely substitute for them, after their fear, security, [for] they worship Me, not associating anything with Me. But whoever disbelieves after that - then those are the defiantly disobedient.

24:56.transliteration: Waaqeemoo assalata waatooazzakata waateeAAoo arrasoolalaAAallakum turhamoon
24:56.translation: And establish prayer and give zakāh and obey the Messenger - that you may receive mercy.

24:57.transliteration: La tahsabanna allatheenakafaroo muAAjizeena fee al-ardi wama/wahumu annaruwalabi/sa almaseer
24:57.translation: Never think that the disbelievers are causing failure [to Allāh] upon the earth. Their refuge will be the Fire - and how wretched the destination.

24:58.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanooliyasta/thinkumu allatheena malakat aymanukumwallatheena lam yablughoo alhuluma minkumthalatha marratin min qabli salatialfajri waheena tadaAAoona thiyabakum mina aththaheeratiwamin baAAdi salati alAAisha-i thalathuAAawratin lakum laysa AAalaykum wala AAalayhim junahunbaAAdahunna tawwafoona AAalaykum baAAdukumAAala baAAdin kathalika yubayyinu Allahulakumu al-ayati wallahu AAaleemun hakeem
24:58.translation: O you who have believed, let those whom your right hands possess and those who have not [yet] reached puberty among you ask permission of you [before entering] at three times: before the dawn prayer and when you put aside your clothing [for rest] at noon and after the night prayer. [These are] three times of privacy for you. There is no blame upon you nor upon them beyond these [periods], for they continually circulate among you - some of you, among others. Thus does Allāh make clear to you the verses [i.e., His ordinances]; and Allāh is Knowing and Wise.

24:59.transliteration: Wa-itha balagha al-atfaluminkumu alhuluma falyasta/thinoo kama ista/thanaallatheena min qablihim kathalika yubayyinu Allahulakum ayatihi wallahu AAaleemun hakeem
24:59.translation: And when the children among you reach puberty, let them ask permission [at all times] as those before them have done. Thus does Allāh make clear to you His verses; and Allāh is Knowing and Wise.

24:60.transliteration: WalqawaAAidu mina annisa-iallatee la yarjoona nikahan falaysaAAalayhinna junahun an yadaAAna thiyabahunnaghayra mutabarrijatin bizeenatin waan yastaAAfifna khayrunlahunna wallahu sameeAAun AAaleem
24:60.translation: And women of post-menstrual age who have no desire for marriage - there is no blame upon them for putting aside their outer garments [but] not displaying adornment. But to modestly refrain [from that] is better for them. And Allāh is Hearing and Knowing.

24:61.transliteration: Laysa AAala al-aAAma harajunwala AAala al-aAAraji harajun walaAAala almareedi harajun wala AAalaanfusikum an ta/kuloo min buyootikum aw buyooti aba-ikumaw buyooti ommahatikum aw buyooti ikhwanikum awbuyooti akhawatikum aw buyooti aAAmamikum awbuyooti AAammatikum aw buyooti akhwalikum awbuyooti khalatikum aw ma malaktum mafatihahuaw sadeeqikum laysa AAalaykum junahun an ta/kuloojameeAAan aw ashtatan fa-itha dakhaltum buyootanfasallimoo AAala anfusikum tahiyyatan min AAindiAllahi mubarakatan tayyibatan kathalikayubayyinu Allahu lakumu al-ayati laAAallakumtaAAqiloon
24:61.translation: There is not upon the blind [any] constraint nor upon the lame constraint nor upon the ill constraint nor upon yourselves when you eat from your [own] houses or the houses of your fathers or the houses of your mothers or the houses of your brothers or the houses of your sisters or the houses of your father's brothers or the houses of your father's sisters or the houses of your mother's brothers or the houses of your mother's sisters or [from houses] whose keys you possess or [from the house] of your friend. There is no blame upon you whether you eat together or separately. But when you enter houses, give greetings of peace upon each other - a greeting from Allāh, blessed and good. Thus does Allāh make clear to you the verses [of ordinance] that you may understand.

24:62.transliteration: Innama almu/minoona allatheenaamanoo billahi warasoolihi wa-itha kanoomaAAahu AAala amrin jamiAAin lam yathhaboo hattayasta/thinoohu inna allatheena yasta/thinoonakaola-ika allatheena yu/minoona billahiwarasoolihi fa-itha ista/thanooka libaAAdisha/nihim fa/than liman shi/ta minhum wastaghfirlahumu Allaha inna Allaha ghafoorun raheem
24:62.translation: The believers are only those who believe in Allāh and His Messenger and, when they are [meeting] with him for a matter of common interest, do not depart until they have asked his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission, [O Muḥammad] - those are the ones who believe in Allāh and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission due to something of their affairs, then give permission to whom you will among them and ask forgiveness for them of Allāh. Indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

24:63.transliteration: La tajAAaloo duAAaa arrasoolibaynakum kaduAAa-i baAAdikum baAAdan qadyaAAlamu Allahu allatheena yatasallaloona minkumliwathan falyahthari allatheena yukhalifoonaAAan amrihi an tuseebahum fitnatun aw yuseebahumAAathabun aleem
24:63.translation: Do not make [your] calling of the Messenger among yourselves as the call of one of you to another. Already Allāh knows those of you who slip away, concealed by others. So let those beware who dissent from his [i.e., the Prophet's] order, lest fitnah strike them or a painful punishment.

24:64.transliteration: Ala inna lillahi ma feeassamawati wal-ardi qadyaAAlamu ma antum AAalayhi wayawma yurjaAAoona ilayhifayunabbi-ohum bima AAamiloo wallahubikulli shay-in AAaleem
24:64.translation: Unquestionably, to Allāh belongs whatever is in the heavens and earth. Already He knows that upon which you [stand] and [knows] the Day when they will be returned to Him and He will inform them of what they have done. And Allāh is Knowing of all things.

25:1.transliteration: Tabaraka allathee nazzalaalfurqana AAala AAabdihi liyakoona lilAAalameenanatheera
25:1.translation: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His Servant that he may be to the worlds a warner -

25:2.transliteration: Allathee lahu mulku assamawatiwal-ardi walam yattakhith waladan walamyakun lahu shareekun fee almulki wakhalaqa kulla shay-infaqaddarahu taqdeera
25:2.translation: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with [precise] determination.

25:3.transliteration: Wattakhathoo min doonihi alihatanla yakhluqoona shay-an wahum yukhlaqoona walayamlikoona li-anfusihim darran wala nafAAan walayamlikoona mawtan wala hayatan walanushoora
25:3.translation: But they have taken besides Him gods which create nothing, while they are created, and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not [power to cause] death or life or resurrection.

25:4.transliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo in hathailla ifkun iftarahu waaAAanahu AAalayhiqawmun akharoona faqad jaoo thulmanwazoora
25:4.translation: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur’ān] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.

25:5.transliteration: Waqaloo asateeru al-awwaleenaiktatabaha fahiya tumla AAalayhi bukratan waaseela
25:5.translation: And they say, "Legends of the former peoples which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."

25:6.transliteration: Qul anzalahu allathee yaAAlamu assirrafee assamawati wal-ardiinnahu kana ghafooran raheema
25:6.translation: Say, [O Muḥammad], "It has been revealed by He who knows [every] secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful."

25:7.transliteration: Waqaloo mali hatha arrasooliya/kulu attaAAama wayamshee fee al-aswaqilawla onzila ilayhi malakun fayakoona maAAahu natheera
25:7.translation: And they say, "What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner?

25:8.transliteration: Aw yulqa ilayhi kanzun aw takoonu lahujannatun ya/kulu minha waqala aththalimoonain tattabiAAoona illa rajulan mashoora
25:8.translation: Or [why is not] a treasure presented to him [from heaven], or does he [not] have a garden from which he eats?" And the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."

25:9.transliteration: Onthur kayfa daraboolaka al-amthala fadalloo fala yastateeAAoonasabeela
25:9.translation: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.

25:10.transliteration: Tabaraka allathee in shaajaAAala laka khayran min thalika jannatin tajreemin tahtiha al-anharu wayajAAal laka qusoora
25:10.translation: Blessed is He who, if He willed, could have made for you [something] better than that - gardens beneath which rivers flow - and could make for you palaces.

25:11.transliteration: Bal kaththaboo bissaAAatiwaaAAtadna liman kaththaba bissaAAatisaAAeera
25:11.translation: But they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.

25:12.transliteration: Itha raat-hum min makaninbaAAeedin samiAAoo laha taghayyuthanwazafeera
25:12.translation: When it [i.e., the Hellfire] sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.

25:13.transliteration: Wa-itha olqoo minha makanandayyiqan muqarraneena daAAaw hunalika thuboora
25:13.translation: And when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains, they will cry out thereupon for destruction.

25:14.transliteration: La tadAAoo alyawma thubooran wahidanwadAAoo thubooran katheera
25:14.translation: [They will be told], "Do not cry this Day for one destruction but cry for much destruction."

25:15.transliteration: Qul athalika khayrun am jannatualkhuldi allatee wuAAida almuttaqoona kanat lahum jazaanwamaseera
25:15.translation: Say, "Is that better or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the righteous? It will be for them a reward and destination.

25:16.transliteration: Lahum feeha ma yashaoonakhalideena kana AAala rabbika waAAdanmas-oola
25:16.translation: For them therein is whatever they wish, [while] abiding eternally. It is ever upon your Lord a promise [worthy to be] requested.

25:17.transliteration: Wayawma yahshuruhum wamayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi fayaqoolu aantum adlaltumAAibadee haola-i am hum dalloo assabeel
25:17.translation: And [mention] the Day He will gather them and that which they worship besides Allāh and will say, "Did you mislead these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?"

25:18.transliteration: Qaloo subhanaka ma kanayanbaghee lana an nattakhitha min doonika min awliyaawalakin mattaAAtahum waabaahum hattanasoo aththikra wakanoo qawman boora
25:18.translation: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies [i.e., protectors]. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."

25:19.transliteration: Faqad kaththabookum bimataqooloona fama tastateeAAoona sarfan walanasran waman yathlim minkum nuthiqhuAAathaban kabeera
25:19.translation: So they will deny you, [disbelievers], in what you say, and you cannot avert [punishment] or [find] help. And whoever commits injustice among you - We will make him taste a great punishment.

25:20.transliteration: Wama arsalna qablaka minaalmursaleena illa innahum laya/kuloona attaAAamawayamshoona fee al-aswaqi wajaAAalna baAAdakumlibaAAdin fitnatan atasbiroona wakanarabbuka baseera
25:20.translation: And We did not send before you, [O Muḥammad], any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience? And ever is your Lord, Seeing.

25:21.transliteration: Waqala allatheena layarjoona liqaana lawla onzila AAalaynaalmala-ikatu aw nara rabbana laqadiistakbaroo fee anfusihim waAAataw AAutuwwan kabeera
25:21.translation: And those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, "Why were not angels sent down to us, or [why] do we [not] see our Lord?" They have certainly become arrogant within themselves and [become] insolent with great insolence.

25:22.transliteration: Yawma yarawna almala-ikata labushra yawma-ithin lilmujrimeena wayaqooloona hijranmahjoora
25:22.translation: The day they see the angels - no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals, and [the angels] will say, "Prevented and inaccessible."

25:23.transliteration: Waqadimna ila maAAamiloo min AAamalin fajaAAalnahu habaan manthoora
25:23.translation: And We will approach [i.e., regard] what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed.

25:24.transliteration: As-habu aljannati yawma-ithinkhayrun mustaqarran waahsanu maqeela
25:24.translation: The companions of Paradise, that Day, are [in] a better settlement and better resting place.

25:25.transliteration: Wayawma tashaqqaqu assamao bilghamamiwanuzzila almala-ikatu tanzeela
25:25.translation: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.

25:26.transliteration: Almulku yawma-ithin alhaqqu lirrahmaniwakana yawman AAala alkafireena AAaseera
25:26.translation: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day.

25:27.transliteration: Wayawma yaAAaddu aththalimuAAala yadayhi yaqoolu ya laytanee ittakhathtumaAAa arrasooli sabeela
25:27.translation: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way.

25:28.transliteration: Ya waylata laytanee lamattakhith fulanan khaleela
25:28.translation: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend.

25:29.transliteration: Laqad adallanee AAani aththikribaAAda ith jaanee wakana ashshaytanulil-insani khathoola
25:29.translation: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter."

25:30.transliteration: Waqala arrasoolu yarabbi inna qawmee ittakhathoo hatha alqur-anamahjoora
25:30.translation: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur’ān as [a thing] abandoned."

25:31.transliteration: Wakathalika jaAAalna likullinabiyyin AAaduwwan mina almujrimeena wakafa birabbika hadiyanwanaseera
25:31.translation: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper.

25:32.transliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lawlanuzzila AAalayhi alqur-anu jumlatan wahidatan kathalikalinuthabbita bihi fu-adaka warattalnahu tarteela
25:32.translation: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur’ān not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.

25:33.transliteration: Wala ya/toonaka bimathalin illaji/naka bilhaqqi waahsana tafseera
25:33.translation: And they do not come to you with an example [i.e., argument] except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.

25:34.transliteration: Allatheena yuhsharoona AAalawujoohihim ila jahannama ola-ika sharrun makananwaadallu sabeela
25:34.translation: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way.

25:35.transliteration: Walaqad atayna moosaalkitaba wajaAAalna maAAahu akhahu haroonawazeera
25:35.translation: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant.

25:36.transliteration: Faqulna ithhaba ilaalqawmi allatheena kaththaboo bi-ayatinafadammarnahum tadmeera
25:36.translation: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction.

25:37.transliteration: Waqawma noohin lamma kaththabooarrusula aghraqnahum wajaAAalnahum linnasiayatan waaAAtadna liththalimeenaAAathaban aleema
25:37.translation: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.

25:38.transliteration: WaAAadan wathamooda waas-habaarrassi waquroonan bayna thalika katheera
25:38.translation: And [We destroyed] ʿAad and Thamūd and the companions of the well and many generations between them.

25:39.transliteration: Wakullan darabna lahu al-amthalawakullan tabbarna tatbeera
25:39.translation: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction.

25:40.transliteration: Walaqad ataw AAala alqaryati allateeomtirat matara assaw-i afalam yakoonooyarawnaha bal kanoo la yarjoona nushoora
25:40.translation: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil [i.e., stones]. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection.

25:41.transliteration: Wa-itha raawka in yattakhithoonakailla huzuwan ahatha allathee baAAatha Allahurasoola
25:41.translation: And when they see you, [O Muḥammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allāh has sent as a messenger?

25:42.transliteration: In kada layudillunaAAan alihatina lawla an sabarnaAAalayha wasawfa yaAAlamoona heena yarawna alAAathabaman adallu sabeela
25:42.translation: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way.

25:43.transliteration: Araayta mani ittakhatha ilahahuhawahu afaanta takoonu AAalayhi wakeela
25:43.translation: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him?

25:44.transliteration: Am tahsabu anna aktharahumyasmaAAoona aw yaAAqiloona in hum illa kal-anAAamibal hum adallu sabeela
25:44.translation: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way.

25:45.transliteration: Alam tara ila rabbika kayfa madda aththillawalaw shaa lajaAAalahu sakinan thumma jaAAalnaashshamsa AAalayhi daleela
25:45.translation: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication.

25:46.translation: Then We [retract and] hold it with Us for a brief grasp.

25:47.transliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala lakumuallayla libasan wannawma subatan wajaAAalaannahara nushoora
25:47.translation: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection.

25:48.transliteration: Wahuwa allathee arsala arriyahabushran bayna yaday rahmatihi waanzalna mina assama-imaan tahoora
25:48.translation: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy [i.e., rainfall], and We send down from the sky pure water

25:49.transliteration: Linuhyiya bihi baldatan maytanwanusqiyahu mimma khalaqna anAAaman waanasiyyakatheera
25:49.translation: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men.

25:50.transliteration: Walaqad sarrafnahu baynahumliyaththakkaroo faaba aktharu annasiilla kufoora
25:50.translation: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.

25:51.transliteration: Walaw shi/na labaAAathna feekulli qaryatin natheera
25:51.translation: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner.

25:52.transliteration: Fala tutiAAi alkafireenawajahidhum bihi jihadan kabeera
25:52.translation: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with it [i.e., the Qur’ān] a great striving.

25:53.transliteration: Wahuwa allathee maraja albahraynihatha AAathbun furatun wahatha milhunojajun wajaAAala baynahuma barzakhan wahijranmahjoora
25:53.translation: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas [i.e., bodies of water], one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition.

25:54.transliteration: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa mina alma-ibasharan fajaAAalahu nasaban wasihran wakanarabbuka qadeera
25:54.translation: And it is He who has created from water [i.e., semen] a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation].

25:55.transliteration: WayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi mala yanfaAAuhum wala yadurruhum wakanaalkafiru AAala rabbihi thaheera
25:55.translation: But they worship rather than Allāh that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan].

25:56.translation: And We have not sent you, [O Muḥammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.

25:57.transliteration: Qul ma as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrinilla man shaa an yattakhitha ilarabbihi sabeela
25:57.translation: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way."

25:58.transliteration: Watawakkal AAala alhayyi allatheela yamootu wasabbih bihamdihi wakafabihi bithunoobi AAibadihi khabeera
25:58.translation: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allāh] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, [fully] Aware -

25:59.transliteration: Allathee khalaqa assamawatiwal-arda wama baynahuma fee sittatiayyamin thumma istawa AAala alAAarshi arrahmanufas-al bihi khabeera
25:59.translation: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed [i.e., the Prophet (ﷺ)].

25:60.transliteration: Wa-itha qeela lahumu osjudoo lirrahmaniqaloo wama arrahmanu anasjudulima ta/muruna wazadahum nufoora
25:60.translation: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion.

25:61.transliteration: Tabaraka allathee jaAAala feeassama-i buroojan wajaAAala feeha sirajanwaqamaran muneera
25:61.translation: Blessed is He who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a [burning] lamp and luminous moon.

25:62.transliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala allayla wannaharakhilfatan liman arada an yaththakkara aw aradashukoora
25:62.translation: And it is He who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.

25:63.transliteration: WaAAibadu arrahmaniallatheena yamshoona AAala al-ardi hawnanwa-itha khatabahumu aljahiloona qaloosalama
25:63.translation: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace,

25:64.translation: And those who spend [part of] the night to their Lord prostrating and standing [in prayer]

25:65.transliteration: Wallatheena yaqooloona rabbanaisrif AAanna AAathaba jahannama inna AAathabahakana gharama
25:65.translation: And those who say, "Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering;

25:66.translation: Indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence."

25:67.transliteration: Wallatheena ithaanfaqoo lam yusrifoo walam yaqturoo wakana bayna thalikaqawama
25:67.translation: And [they are] those who, when they spend, do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever, between that, [justly] moderate

25:68.transliteration: Wallatheena layadAAoona maAAa Allahi ilahan akharawala yaqtuloona annafsa allatee harrama Allahuilla bilhaqqi wala yaznoona wamanyafAAal thalika yalqa athama
25:68.translation: And those who do not invoke with Allāh another deity or kill the soul which Allāh has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.

25:69.transliteration: YudaAAaf lahu alAAathabu yawmaalqiyamati wayakhlud feehi muhana
25:69.translation: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated -

25:70.transliteration: Illa man taba waamanawaAAamila AAamalan salihan faola-ikayubaddilu Allahu sayyi-atihim hasanatinwakana Allahu ghafooran raheema
25:70.translation: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them Allāh will replace their evil deeds with good. And ever is Allāh Forgiving and Merciful.

25:71.transliteration: Waman taba waAAamila salihanfa-innahu yatoobu ila Allahi mataba
25:71.translation: And he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to Allāh with [accepted] repentance.

25:72.transliteration: Wallatheena layashhadoona azzoora wa-itha marroo billaghwimarroo kirama
25:72.translation: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.

25:73.transliteration: Wallatheena itha thukkiroobi-ayati rabbihim lam yakhirroo AAalayha summanwaAAumyana
25:73.translation: And those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind.

25:74.transliteration: Wallatheena yaqooloona rabbanahab lana min azwajina wathurriyyatinaqurrata aAAyunin wajAAalna lilmuttaqeena imama
25:74.translation: And those who say, "Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us a leader [i.e., example] for the righteous."

25:75.transliteration: Ola-ika yujzawna alghurfata bimasabaroo wayulaqqawna feeha tahiyyatan wasalama
25:75.translation: Those will be awarded the Chamber for what they patiently endured, and they will be received therein with greetings and [words of] peace,

25:76.translation: Abiding eternally therein. Good is the settlement and residence.

25:77.transliteration: Qul ma yaAAbao bikum rabbee lawladuAAaokum faqad kaththabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizama
25:77.translation: Say, "What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?" For you [disbelievers] have denied, so it [i.e., your denial] is going to be adherent.

26:2.translation: These are the verses of the clear Book.

26:3.translation: Perhaps, [O Muḥammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.

26:4.transliteration: In nasha/ nunazzil AAalayhim mina assama-iayatan fathallat aAAnaquhum lahakhadiAAeen
26:4.translation: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.

26:5.transliteration: Wama ya/teehim min thikrin minaarrahmani muhdathin illa kanooAAanhu muAArideen
26:5.translation: And no mention [i.e., revelation] comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.

26:6.transliteration: Faqad kaththaboo fasaya/teehim anbaoma kanoo bihi yastahzi-oon
26:6.translation: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.

26:7.transliteration: Awa lam yaraw ila al-ardi kamanbatna feeha min kulli zawjin kareem
26:7.translation: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?

26:8.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:8.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:9.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:10.transliteration: Wa-ith nada rabbukamoosa ani i/ti alqawma aththalimeen
26:10.translation: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -

26:11.translation: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allāh?"

26:12.translation: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me

26:13.transliteration: Wayadeequ sadree walayantaliqu lisanee faarsil ila haroon
26:13.translation: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.

26:14.translation: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."

26:15.transliteration: Qala kalla fathhababi-ayatina inna maAAakum mustamiAAoon
26:15.translation: [Allāh] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.

26:16.transliteration: Fa/tiya firAAawna faqoola innarasoolu rabbi alAAalameen
26:16.translation: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,

26:17.translation: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"

26:18.transliteration: Qala alam nurabbika feenawaleedan walabithta feena min AAumurika sineen
26:18.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?

26:19.transliteration: WafaAAalta faAAlataka allatee faAAaltawaanta mina alkafireen
26:19.translation: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."

26:20.translation: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray [i.e., ignorant].

26:21.transliteration: Fafarartu minkum lamma khiftukumfawahaba lee rabbee hukman wajaAAalanee mina almursaleen
26:21.translation: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me judgement [i.e., wisdom and prophet hood] and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.

26:22.transliteration: Watilka niAAmatun tamunnuha AAalayyaan AAabbadta banee isra-eel
26:22.translation: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"

26:23.translation: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"

26:24.transliteration: Qala rabbu assamawatiwal-ardi wama baynahuma in kuntummooqineen
26:24.translation: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."

26:25.translation: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"

26:26.translation: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."

26:27.transliteration: Qala inna rasoolakumu allatheeorsila ilaykum lamajnoon
26:27.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."

26:28.transliteration: Qala rabbu almashriqi walmaghribiwama baynahuma in kuntum taAAqiloon
26:28.translation: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."

26:29.transliteration: Qala la-ini ittakhathta ilahanghayree laajAAalannaka mina almasjooneen
26:29.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."

26:30.transliteration: Qala awa law ji/tuka bishay-in mubeen
26:30.translation: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you something [i.e., proof] manifest?"

26:31.transliteration: Qala fa/ti bihi in kunta mina assadiqeen
26:31.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."

26:32.translation: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.

26:33.translation: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.

26:34.transliteration: Qala lilmala-i hawlahu inna hathalasahirun AAaleem
26:34.translation: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.

26:35.transliteration: Yureedu an yukhrijakum min ardikumbisihrihi famatha ta/muroon
26:35.translation: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"

26:36.transliteration: Qaloo arjih waakhahu wabAAathfee almada-ini hashireen
26:36.translation: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers

26:37.translation: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."

26:38.translation: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.

26:39.translation: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate.

26:40.transliteration: LaAAallana nattabiAAu assaharatain kanoo humu alghalibeen
26:40.translation: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"

26:41.transliteration: Falamma jaa assaharatuqaloo lifirAAawna a-inna lana laajran in kunnanahnu alghalibeen
26:41.translation: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

26:42.translation: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."

26:43.transliteration: Qala lahum moosa alqoo maantum mulqoon
26:43.translation: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

26:44.transliteration: Faalqaw hibalahum waAAisiyyahumwaqaloo biAAizzati firAAawna inna lanahnualghaliboon
26:44.translation: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."

26:45.transliteration: Faalqa moosa AAasahufa-itha hiya talqafu ma ya/fikoon
26:45.translation: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

26:46.translation: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allāh].

26:47.translation: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,

26:48.translation: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."

26:49.transliteration: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athanalakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu assihrafalasawfa taAAlamoona laoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakummin khilafin walaosallibannakum ajmaAAeen
26:49.translation: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed him [i.e., Moses] before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."

26:50.transliteration: Qaloo la dayra innaila rabbina munqaliboon
26:50.translation: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.

26:51.transliteration: Inna natmaAAu an yaghfira lanarabbuna khatayana an kunnaawwala almu/mineen
26:51.translation: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."

26:52.transliteration: Waawhayna ila moosaan asri biAAibadee innakum muttabaAAoon
26:52.translation: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."

26:53.translation: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers

26:54.translation: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,

26:55.translation: And indeed, they are enraging us,

26:56.translation: And indeed, we are a cautious society..."

26:57.translation: So We removed them from gardens and springs

26:59.translation: Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.

26:60.translation: So they pursued them at sunrise.

26:61.transliteration: Falamma taraa aljamAAaniqala as-habu moosa innalamudrakoon
26:61.translation: And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, "Indeed, we are to be overtaken!"

26:62.translation: [Moses] said, "No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me."

26:63.transliteration: Faawhayna ila moosaani idrib biAAasaka albahra fanfalaqafakana kullu firqin kattawdi alAAatheem
26:63.translation: Then We inspired to Moses, "Strike with your staff the sea," and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.

26:64.translation: And We advanced thereto the others [i.e., the pursuers].

26:65.translation: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.

26:67.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:67.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:68.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:69.translation: And recite to them the news of Abraham,

26:70.translation: When he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?"

26:71.translation: They said, "We worship idols and remain to them devoted."

26:72.translation: He said, "Do they hear you when you supplicate?

26:73.translation: Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?"

26:74.translation: They said, "But we found our fathers doing thus."

26:75.translation: He said, "Then do you see what you have been worshipping,

26:77.translation: Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,

26:78.translation: Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.

26:79.translation: And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.

26:80.translation: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me

26:81.translation: And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life

26:82.transliteration: Wallathee atmaAAu anyaghfira lee khatee-atee yawma addeen
26:82.translation: And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense."

26:83.translation: [And he said], "My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.

26:84.translation: And grant me a mention [i.e., reputation] of honor among later generations.

26:85.translation: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.

26:86.translation: And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.

26:87.translation: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -

26:88.translation: The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children

26:89.translation: But only one who comes to Allāh with a sound heart."

26:90.translation: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.

26:91.translation: And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,

26:92.translation: And it will be said to them, "Where are those you used to worship

26:93.transliteration: Min dooni Allahi hal yansuroonakumaw yantasiroon
26:93.translation: Other than Allāh? Can they help you or help themselves?"

26:94.translation: So they will be overturned into it [i.e., Hellfire], they and the deviators

26:95.translation: And the soldiers of Iblees, all together.

26:96.translation: They will say while they dispute therein,

26:97.translation: "By Allāh, we were indeed in manifest error

26:98.translation: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.

26:99.translation: And no one misguided us except the criminals.

26:100.translation: So now we have no intercessors

26:102.transliteration: Falaw anna lana karratan fanakoonamina almu/mineen
26:102.translation: Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers..."

26:103.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:103.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:104.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:105.translation: The people of Noah denied the messengers

26:106.transliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum noohunala tattaqoon
26:106.translation: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you not fear Allāh?

26:107.translation: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

26:108.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:109.transliteration: Wama as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin inajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameen
26:109.translation: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

26:110.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me."

26:111.translation: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"

26:112.translation: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?

26:113.transliteration: In hisabuhum illa AAalarabbee law tashAAuroon
26:113.translation: Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.

26:114.translation: And I am not one to drive away the believers.

26:115.translation: I am only a clear warner."

26:116.transliteration: Qaloo la-in lam tantahi yanoohu latakoonanna mina almarjoomeen
26:116.translation: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."

26:117.translation: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.

26:118.transliteration: Faftah baynee wabaynahum fathanwanajjinee waman maAAiya mina almu/mineen
26:118.translation: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."

26:119.translation: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

26:120.translation: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.

26:121.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:121.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:122.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:124.transliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohumhoodun ala tattaqoon
26:124.translation: When their brother Hūd said to them, "Will you not fear Allāh?

26:125.translation: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

26:126.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:127.transliteration: Wama as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin inajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameen
26:127.translation: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

26:128.translation: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,

26:129.translation: And take for yourselves constructions [i.e., palaces and fortresses] that you might abide eternally?

26:130.translation: And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.

26:131.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:132.translation: And fear He who provided you with that which you know,

26:133.translation: Provided you with grazing livestock and children

26:135.translation: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."

26:136.transliteration: Qaloo sawaon AAalaynaawaAAathta am lam takun mina alwaAAitheen
26:136.translation: They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.

26:137.translation: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,

26:138.translation: And we are not to be punished."

26:139.transliteration: Fakaththaboohu faahlaknahuminna fee thalika laayatan wama kanaaktharuhum mu/mineen
26:139.translation: And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:140.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:142.transliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum salihunala tattaqoon
26:142.translation: When their brother Ṣāliḥ said to them, "Will you not fear Allāh?

26:143.translation: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

26:144.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:145.transliteration: Wama as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin inajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameen
26:145.translation: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

26:146.translation: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],

26:148.translation: And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?

26:149.translation: And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.

26:150.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:151.translation: And do not obey the order of the transgressors,

26:152.translation: Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend."

26:153.translation: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.

26:154.transliteration: Ma anta illa basharun mithlunafa/ti bi-ayatin in kunta mina assadiqeena
26:154.translation: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful."

26:155.transliteration: Qala hathihi naqatunlaha shirbun walakum shirbu yawmin maAAloom
26:155.translation: He said, "This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.

26:156.transliteration: Wala tamassooha bisoo-infaya/khuthakum AAathabu yawmin AAatheem
26:156.translation: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day."

26:157.translation: But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.

26:158.transliteration: Faakhathahumu alAAathabu innafee thalika laayatan wama kanaaktharuhum mu/mineen
26:158.translation: And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:159.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:160.translation: The people of Lot denied the messengers

26:161.transliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum lootunala tattaqoon
26:161.translation: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear Allāh?

26:162.translation: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

26:163.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:164.transliteration: Wama as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin inajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameen
26:164.translation: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

26:165.translation: Do you approach males among the worlds

26:166.transliteration: Watatharoona ma khalaqa lakumrabbukum min azwajikum bal antum qawmun AAadoon
26:166.translation: And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing."

26:167.transliteration: Qaloo la-in lam tantahi yalootu latakoonanna mina almukhrajeen
26:167.translation: They said, "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted."

26:168.translation: He said, "Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].

26:169.translation: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do."

26:170.translation: So We saved him and his family, all,

26:171.translation: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.

26:173.translation: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.

26:174.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:174.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:175.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:176.translation: The companions of the thicket [i.e., the people of Madyan] denied the messengers

26:177.translation: When Shuʿayb said to them, "Will you not fear Allāh?

26:178.translation: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

26:179.translation: So fear Allāh and obey me.

26:180.transliteration: Wama as-alukum AAalayhi min ajrin inajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameen
26:180.translation: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

26:181.translation: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.

26:182.translation: And weigh with an even [i.e., honest] balance.

26:183.transliteration: Wala tabkhasoo annasaashyaahum wala taAAthaw fee al-ardimufsideen
26:183.translation: And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.

26:184.translation: And fear He who created you and the former creation."

26:185.translation: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.

26:186.transliteration: Wama anta illa basharunmithluna wa-in nathunnuka lamina alkathibeen
26:186.translation: You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.

26:187.transliteration: Faasqit AAalayna kisafan minaassama-i in kunta mina assadiqeen
26:187.translation: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful."

26:188.translation: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."

26:189.transliteration: Fakaththaboohu faakhathahumAAathabu yawmi aththullati innahu kanaAAathaba yawmin AAatheem
26:189.translation: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.

26:190.transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wamakana aktharuhum mu/mineen
26:190.translation: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

26:191.translation: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

26:192.translation: And indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.

26:193.translation: The Trustworthy Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] has brought it down

26:194.translation: Upon your heart, [O Muḥammad] - that you may be of the warners -

26:196.translation: And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.

26:197.transliteration: Awa lam yakun lahum ayatan anyaAAlamahu AAulamao banee isra-eel
26:197.translation: And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?

26:198.translation: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners

26:199.translation: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.

26:200.translation: Thus have We inserted it [i.e., disbelief] into the hearts of the criminals.

26:201.transliteration: La yu/minoona bihi hattayarawoo alAAathaba al-aleem
26:201.translation: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.

26:202.translation: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.

26:203.translation: And they will say, "May we be reprieved?"

26:204.translation: So for Our punishment are they impatient?

26:205.translation: Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years

26:206.translation: And then there came to them that which they were promised?

26:207.translation: They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.

26:208.transliteration: Wama ahlakna min qaryatin illalaha munthiroon
26:208.translation: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners

26:209.translation: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.

26:210.translation: And the devils have not brought it [i.e., the revelation] down.

26:211.translation: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.

26:212.translation: Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.

26:213.transliteration: Fala tadAAu maAAa Allahi ilahanakhara fatakoona mina almuAAaththabeen
26:213.translation: So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

26:214.translation: And warn, [O Muḥammad], your closest kindred.

26:215.translation: And lower your wing [i.e., show kindness] to those who follow you of the believers.

26:216.transliteration: Fa-in AAasawka faqul innee baree-onmimma taAAmaloon
26:216.translation: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."

26:217.translation: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,

26:218.translation: Who sees you when you arise

26:219.translation: And your movement among those who prostrate.

26:220.translation: Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

26:221.translation: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?

26:222.translation: They descend upon every sinful liar.

26:223.translation: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.

26:224.translation: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;

26:225.transliteration: Alam tara annahum fee kulli wadinyaheemoon
26:225.translation: Do you not see that in every valley they roam

26:226.translation: And that they say what they do not do? -

26:227.transliteration: Illa allatheena amanoowaAAamiloo assalihati wathakaroo Allahakatheeran wantasaroo min baAAdi ma thulimoowasayaAAlamu allatheena thalamoo ayyamunqalabin yanqaliboon
26:227.translation: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allāh often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.

27:1.translation: Ṭā, Seen. These are the verses of the Qur’ān [i.e., recitation] and a clear Book

27:2.translation: As guidance and good tidings for the believers

27:3.transliteration: Allatheena yuqeemoona assalatawayu/toona azzakata wahum bil-akhiratihum yooqinoon
27:3.translation: Who establish prayer and give zakāh, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].

27:4.transliteration: Inna allatheena la yu/minoonabil-akhirati zayyanna lahum aAAmalahumfahum yaAAmahoon
27:4.translation: Indeed, for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made pleasing to them their deeds, so they wander blindly.

27:5.transliteration: Ola-ika allatheena lahum soo-oalAAathabi wahum fee al-akhirati humu al-akhsaroon
27:5.translation: Those are the ones for whom there will be the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they are the greatest losers.

27:6.transliteration: Wa-innaka latulaqqa alqur-anamin ladun hakeemin AAaleem
27:6.translation: And indeed, [O Muḥammad], you receive the Qur’ān from one Wise and Knowing.

27:7.transliteration: Ith qala moosa li-ahlihiinnee anastu naran saateekum minhabikhabarin aw ateekum bishihabin qabasinlaAAallakum tastaloon
27:7.translation: [Mention] when Moses said to his family, "Indeed, I have perceived a fire. I will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves."

27:8.transliteration: Falamma jaaha noodiya anboorika man fee annari waman hawlahawasubhana Allahi rabbi alAAalameen
27:8.translation: But when he came to it, he was called, "Blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it. And exalted is Allāh, Lord of the worlds.

27:9.transliteration: Ya moosa innahu ana AllahualAAazeezu alhakeem
27:9.translation: O Moses, indeed it is I - Allāh, the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

27:10.transliteration: Waalqi AAasaka falamma raahatahtazzu kaannaha jannun walla mudbiranwalam yuAAaqqib ya moosa la takhaf innee layakhafu ladayya almursaloon
27:10.translation: And [he was told], "Throw down your staff." But when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allāh said], "O Moses, fear not. Indeed, in My presence the messengers do not fear.

27:11.transliteration: Illa man thalama thummabaddala husnan baAAda soo-in fa-innee ghafoorun raheem
27:11.translation: Otherwise, he who wrongs, then substitutes good after evil - indeed, I am Forgiving and Merciful.

27:12.transliteration: Waadkhil yadaka fee jaybika takhruj baydaamin ghayri soo-in fee tisAAi ayatin ilafirAAawna waqawmihi innahum kanoo qawman fasiqeen
27:12.translation: And put your hand into the opening of your garment [at the breast]; it will come out white without disease. [These are] among the nine signs [you will take] to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."

27:13.transliteration: Falamma jaat-hum ayatunamubsiratan qaloo hatha sihrun mubeen
27:13.translation: But when there came to them Our visible signs, they said, "This is obvious magic."

27:14.transliteration: Wajahadoo biha wastayqanat-haanfusuhum thulman waAAuluwwan fanthurkayfa kana AAaqibatu almufsideen
27:14.translation: And they rejected them, while their [inner] selves were convinced thereof, out of injustice and haughtiness. So see how was the end of the corrupters.

27:15.transliteration: Walaqad atayna dawoodawasulaymana AAilman waqala alhamdulillahi allathee faddalana AAalakatheerin min AAibadihi almu/mineen
27:15.translation: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, "Praise [is due] to Allāh, who has favored us over many of His believing servants."

27:16.transliteration: Wawaritha sulaymanu dawoodawaqala ya ayyuha annasuAAullimna mantiqa attayri waooteenamin kulli shay-in inna hatha lahuwa alfadlualmubeen
27:16.translation: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."

27:17.transliteration: Wahushira lisulaymanajunooduhu mina aljinni wal-insi wattayrifahum yoozaAAoon
27:17.translation: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows

27:18.transliteration: Hatta itha ataw AAalawadi annamli qalat namlatun ya ayyuhaannamlu odkhuloo masakinakum la yahtimannakumsulaymanu wajunooduhu wahum la yashAAuroon
27:18.translation: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not."

27:19.transliteration: Fatabassama dahikan min qawlihawaqala rabbi awziAAnee an ashkura niAAmataka allateeanAAamta AAalayya waAAala walidayya waan aAAmala salihantardahu waadkhilnee birahmatika fee AAibadikaassaliheen
27:19.translation: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."

27:20.transliteration: Watafaqqada attayra faqalama liya la ara alhudhuda am kana minaalgha-ibeen
27:20.translation: And he took attendance of the birds and said, "Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?

27:21.transliteration: LaoAAaththibannahu AAathabanshadeedan aw laathbahannahu aw laya/tiyannee bisultaninmubeen
27:21.translation: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization."

27:22.transliteration: Famakatha ghayra baAAeedin faqala ahattubima lam tuhit bihi waji/tuka min saba-inbinaba-in yaqeen
27:22.translation: But he [i.e., the hoopoe] stayed not long and said, "I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.

27:23.transliteration: Innee wajadtu imraatan tamlikuhum waootiyatmin kulli shay-in walaha AAarshun AAatheem
27:23.translation: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.

27:24.transliteration: Wajadtuha waqawmaha yasjudoonalishshamsi min dooni Allahi wazayyana lahumu ashshaytanuaAAmalahum fasaddahum AAani assabeeli fahumla yahtadoon
27:24.translation: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allāh, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,

27:25.transliteration: Alla yasjudoo lillahi allatheeyukhriju alkhabaa fee assamawati wal-ardiwayaAAlamu ma tukhfoona wama tuAAlinoon
27:25.translation: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allāh, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -

27:26.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illahuwa rabbu alAAarshi alAAatheem
27:26.translation: Allāh - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne."

27:27.transliteration: Qala sananthuru asadaqtaam kunta mina alkathibeen
27:27.translation: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.

27:28.transliteration: Ithhab bikitabee hathafaalqih ilayhim thumma tawalla AAanhum fanthurmatha yarjiAAoon
27:28.translation: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return."

27:29.transliteration: Qalat ya ayyuha almalaoinnee olqiya ilayya kitabun kareem
27:29.translation: She said, "O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.

27:30.transliteration: Innahu min sulaymana wa-innahu bismiAllahi arrahmani arraheem
27:30.translation: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it is [i.e., reads]: 'In the name of Allāh, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,

27:31.translation: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].'"

27:32.transliteration: Qalat ya ayyuha almalaoaftoonee fee amree ma kuntu qatiAAatan amran hattatashhadoon
27:32.translation: She said, "O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me."

27:33.transliteration: Qaloo nahnu oloo quwwatinwaoloo ba/sin shadeedin wal-amru ilayki fanthureematha ta/mureen
27:33.translation: They said, "We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command."

27:34.transliteration: Qalat inna almulooka ithadakhaloo qaryatan afsadooha wajaAAaloo aAAizzata ahlihaathillatan wakathalika yafAAaloon
