64:2.translation: It is He who created you, and among you is the disbeliever, and among you is the believer. And Allāh, of what you do, is Seeing.

64:3.transliteration: Khalaqa assamawati wal-ardabilhaqqi wasawwarakum faahsana suwarakumwa-ilayhi almaseer
64:3.translation: He created the heavens and earth in truth and formed you and perfected your forms; and to Him is the [final] destination.

64:4.transliteration: YaAAlamu ma fee assamawatiwal-ardi wayaAAlamu ma tusirroona wamatuAAlinoona wallahu AAaleemun bithati assudoor
64:4.translation: He knows what is within the heavens and earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare. And Allāh is Knowing of that within the breasts.

64:5.transliteration: Alam ya/tikum nabao allatheena kafaroomin qablu fathaqoo wabala amrihim walahum AAathabunaleem
64:5.translation: Has there not come to you the news of those who disbelieved before? So they tasted the bad consequence of their affair, and they will have a painful punishment.

64:6.transliteration: Thalika bi-annahu kanatta/teehim rusuluhum bilbayyinati faqalooabasharun yahdoonana fakafaroo watawallaw wastaghnaAllahu wallahu ghaniyyun hameed
64:6.translation: That is because their messengers used to come to them with clear evidences, but they said, "Shall human beings guide us?" and disbelieved and turned away. And Allāh dispensed [with them]; and Allāh is Free of need and Praiseworthy.

64:7.transliteration: ZaAAama allatheena kafaroo an lanyubAAathoo qul bala warabbee latubAAathunna thummalatunabbaonna bima AAamiltum wathalika AAalaAllahi yaseer
64:7.translation: Those who disbelieve have claimed that they will never be resurrected. Say, "Yes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected; then you will surely be informed of what you did. And that, for Allāh, is easy."

64:8.transliteration: Faaminoo billahiwarasoolihi wannoori allathee anzalna wallahubima taAAmaloona khabeer
64:8.translation: So believe in Allāh and His Messenger and the light [i.e., the Qur’ān] which We have sent down. And Allāh is Aware of what you do.

64:9.transliteration: Yawma yajmaAAukum liyawmi aljamAAi thalikayawmu attaghabuni waman yu/min billahiwayaAAmal salihan yukaffir AAanhu sayyi-atihiwayudkhilhu jannatin tajree min tahtihaal-anharu khalideena feeha abadan thalikaalfawzu alAAatheem
64:9.translation: The Day He will assemble you for the Day of Assembly - that is the Day of Deprivation. And whoever believes in Allāh and does righteousness - He will remove from him his misdeeds and admit him to gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever. That is the great attainment.

64:10.transliteration: Wallatheena kafaroo wakaththaboobi-ayatina ola-ika as-habuannari khalideena feeha wabi/sa almaseer
64:10.translation: But the ones who disbelieved and denied Our verses - those are the companions of the Fire, abiding eternally therein; and wretched is the destination.

64:11.transliteration: Ma asaba min museebatinilla bi-ithni Allahi waman yu/min billahiyahdi qalbahu wallahu bikulli shay-in AAaleem
64:11.translation: No disaster strikes except by permission of Allāh. And whoever believes in Allāh - He will guide his heart. And Allāh is Knowing of all things.

64:12.transliteration: WaateeAAoo Allaha waateeAAooarrasoola fa-in tawallaytum fa-innama AAalarasoolina albalaghu almubeen
64:12.translation: And obey Allāh and obey the Messenger; but if you turn away - then upon Our Messenger is only [the duty of] clear notification.

64:13.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illahuwa waAAala Allahi falyatawakkali almu/minoon
64:13.translation: Allāh - there is no deity except Him. And upon Allāh let the believers rely.

64:14.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanooinna min azwajikum waawladikum AAaduwwan lakum fahtharoohumwa-in taAAfoo watasfahoo wataghfiroo fa-inna Allahaghafoorun raheem
64:14.translation: O you who have believed, indeed, among your spouses and your children are enemies to you, so beware of them. But if you pardon and overlook and forgive - then indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

64:15.transliteration: Innama amwalukum waawladukumfitnatun wallahu AAindahu ajrun AAatheem
64:15.translation: Your wealth and your children are but a trial, and Allāh has with Him a great reward.

64:16.transliteration: Fattaqoo Allaha maistataAAtum wasmaAAoo waateeAAoo waanfiqookhayran li-anfusikum waman yooqa shuhha nafsihi faola-ikahumu almuflihoon
64:16.translation: So fear Allāh as much as you are able and listen and obey and spend [in the way of Allāh]; it is better for your selves. And whoever is protected from the stinginess of his soul - it is those who will be the successful.

64:17.transliteration: In tuqridoo Allaha qardanhasanan yudaAAifhu lakum wayaghfir lakum wallahushakoorun haleem
64:17.translation: If you loan Allāh a goodly loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. And Allāh is [most] Appreciative and Forbearing,

64:18.translation: Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

65:1.transliteration: Ya ayyuha annabiyyu ithatallaqtumu annisaa fatalliqoohunnaliAAiddatihinna waahsoo alAAiddata wattaqoo Allaharabbakum la tukhrijoohunna min buyootihinna walayakhrujna illa an ya/teena bifahishatinmubayyinatin watilka hudoodu Allahi wamanyataAAadda hudooda Allahi faqad thalamanafsahu la tadree laAAalla Allaha yuhdithubaAAda thalika amra
65:1.translation: O Prophet, when you [Muslims] divorce women, divorce them for [the commencement of] their waiting period and keep count of the waiting period, and fear Allāh, your Lord. Do not turn them out of their [husbands'] houses, nor should they [themselves] leave [during that period] unless they are committing a clear immorality. And those are the limits [set by] Allāh. And whoever transgresses the limits of Allāh has certainly wronged himself. You know not; perhaps Allāh will bring about after that a [different] matter.

65:2.transliteration: Fa-itha balaghna ajalahunnafaamsikoohunna bimaAAroofin aw fariqoohunna bimaAAroofinwaashhidoo thaway AAadlin minkum waaqeemoo ashshahadatalillahi thalikum yooAAathu bihi man kanayu/minu billahi walyawmi al-akhiriwaman yattaqi Allaha yajAAal lahu makhraja
65:2.translation: And when they have [nearly] fulfilled their term, either retain them according to acceptable terms or part with them according to acceptable terms. And bring to witness two just men from among you and establish the testimony for [the acceptance of] Allāh. That is instructed to whoever should believe in Allāh and the Last Day. And whoever fears Allāh - He will make for him a way out

65:3.transliteration: Wayarzuqhu min haythu la yahtasibuwaman yatawakkal AAala Allahi fahuwa hasbuhuinna Allaha balighu amrihi qad jaAAala Allahulikulli shay-in qadra
65:3.translation: And will provide for him from where he does not expect. And whoever relies upon Allāh - then He is sufficient for him. Indeed, Allāh will accomplish His purpose. Allāh has already set for everything a [decreed] extent.

65:4.transliteration: Walla-ee ya-isna mina almaheedimin nisa-ikum ini irtabtum faAAiddatuhunna thalathatuashhurin walla-ee lam yahidna waolatual-ahmali ajaluhunna an yadaAAna hamlahunnawaman yattaqi Allaha yajAAal lahu min amrihi yusra
65:4.translation: And those who no longer expect menstruation among your women - if you doubt, then their period is three months, and [also for] those who have not menstruated. And for those who are pregnant, their term is until they give birth. And whoever fears Allāh - He will make for him of his matter ease.

65:5.transliteration: Thalika amru Allahi anzalahuilaykum waman yattaqi Allaha yukaffir AAanhu sayyi-atihiwayuAAthim lahu ajra
65:5.translation: That is the command of Allāh, which He has sent down to you; and whoever fears Allāh - He will remove for him his misdeeds and make great for him his reward.

65:6.transliteration: Askinoohunna min haythu sakantum minwujdikum wala tudarroohunna litudayyiqooAAalayhinna wa-in kunna olati hamlin faanfiqooAAalayhinna hatta yadaAAna hamlahunnafa-in ardaAAna lakum faatoohunna ojoorahunnawa/tamiroo baynakum bimaAAroofin wa-in taAAasartum fasaturdiAAulahu okhra
65:6.translation: Lodge them [in a section] of where you dwell out of your means and do not harm them in order to oppress them. And if they should be pregnant, then spend on them until they give birth. And if they breastfeed for you, then give them their payment and confer among yourselves in the acceptable way; but if you are in discord, then there may breastfeed for him [i.e., the father] another woman.

65:7.transliteration: Liyunfiq thoo saAAatin min saAAatihiwaman qudira AAalayhi rizquhu falyunfiq mimma atahuAllahu la yukallifu Allahu nafsan illama ataha sayajAAalu AllahubaAAda AAusrin yusra
65:7.translation: Let a man of wealth spend from his wealth, and he whose provision is restricted - let him spend from what Allāh has given him. Allāh does not charge a soul except [according to] what He has given it. Allāh will bring about, after hardship, ease [i.e., relief].

65:8.transliteration: Wakaayyin min qaryatin AAatat AAan amrirabbiha warusulihi fahasabnaha hisabanshadeedan waAAaththabnaha AAathabannukra
65:8.translation: And how many a city was insolent toward the command of its Lord and His messengers, so We took it to severe account and punished it with a terrible punishment.

65:9.transliteration: Fathaqat wabala amrihawakana AAaqibatu amriha khusra
65:9.translation: And it tasted the bad consequence of its affair [i.e., rebellion], and the outcome of its affair was loss.

65:10.transliteration: aAAadda Allahu lahum AAathabanshadeedan fattaqoo Allaha ya olee al-albabiallatheena amanoo qad anzala Allahu ilaykum thikra
65:10.translation: Allāh has prepared for them a severe punishment; so fear Allāh, O you of understanding who have believed. Allāh has sent down to you a message [i.e., the Qur’ān].

65:11.transliteration: Rasoolan yatloo AAalaykum ayatiAllahi mubayyinatin liyukhrija allatheena amanoowaAAamiloo assalihati mina aththulumatiila annoori waman yu/min billahiwayaAAmal salihan yudkhilhu jannatin tajreemin tahtiha al-anharu khalideena feehaabadan qad ahsana Allahu lahu rizqa
65:11.translation: [He sent] a Messenger [i.e., Muḥammad (ﷺ)] reciting to you the distinct verses of Allāh that He may bring out those who believe and do righteous deeds from darknesses into the light. And whoever believes in Allāh and does righteousness - He will admit him into gardens beneath which rivers flow to abide therein forever. Allāh will have perfected for him a provision.

65:12.transliteration: Allahu allathee khalaqa sabAAasamawatin wamina al-ardi mithlahunnayatanazzalu al-amru baynahunna litaAAlamoo anna AllahaAAala kulli shay-in qadeerun waanna Allaha qad ahatabikulli shay-in AAilma
65:12.translation: It is Allāh who has created seven heavens and of the earth, the like of them. [His] command descends among them so you may know that Allāh is over all things competent and that Allāh has encompassed all things in knowledge.

66:1.transliteration: Ya ayyuha annabiyyulima tuharrimu ma ahalla Allahu lakatabtaghee mardata azwajika wallahughafoorun raheem
66:1.translation: O Prophet, why do you prohibit [yourself from] what Allāh has made lawful for you, seeking the approval of your wives? And Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

66:2.transliteration: Qad farada Allahu lakum tahillataaymanikum wallahu mawlakum wahuwaalAAaleemu alhakeem
66:2.translation: Allāh has already ordained for you [Muslims] the dissolution of your oaths. And Allāh is your protector, and He is the Knowing, the Wise.

66:3.transliteration: Wa-ith asarra annabiyyu ilabaAAdi azwajihi hadeethan falammanabbaat bihi waathharahu Allahu AAalayhiAAarrafa baAAdahu waaAArada AAan baAAdinfalamma nabbaaha bihi qalat man anbaaka hathaqala nabbaaniya alAAaleemu alkhabeer
66:3.translation: And [remember] when the Prophet confided to one of his wives a statement; and when she informed [another] of it and Allāh showed it to him, he made known part of it and ignored a part. And when he informed her about it, she said, "Who told you this?" He said, "I was informed by the Knowing, the Aware."

66:4.transliteration: In tatooba ila Allahifaqad saghat quloobukuma wa-in tathaharaAAalayhi fa-inna Allaha huwa mawlahu wajibreelu wasalihualmu/mineena walmala-ikatu baAAda thalika thaheer
66:4.translation: If you two [wives] repent to Allāh, [it is best], for your hearts have deviated. But if you cooperate against him - then indeed Allāh is his protector, and Gabriel and the righteous of the believers and the angels, moreover, are [his] assistants.

66:5.transliteration: AAasa rabbuhu in tallaqakunnaan yubdilahu azwajan khayran minkunna muslimatinmu/minatin qanitatin ta-ibatinAAabidatin sa-ihatin thayyibatinwaabkara
66:5.translation: Perhaps his Lord, if he divorced you [all], would substitute for him wives better than you - submitting [to Allāh], believing, devoutly obedient, repentant, worshipping, and traveling - [ones] previously married and virgins.

66:6.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanooqoo anfusakum waahleekum naran waqooduha annasuwalhijaratu AAalayha mala-ikatunghilathun shidadun la yaAAsoonaAllaha ma amarahum wayafAAaloona mayu/maroon
66:6.translation: O you who have believed, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, over which are [appointed] angels, harsh and severe; they do not disobey Allāh in what He commands them but do what they are commanded.

66:7.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheenakafaroo la taAAtathiroo alyawma innamatujzawna ma kuntum taAAmaloon
66:7.translation: O you who have disbelieved, make no excuses that Day. You will only be recompensed for what you used to do.

66:8.transliteration: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanootooboo ila Allahi tawbatan nasoohanAAasa rabbukum an yukaffira AAankum sayyi-atikumwayudkhilakum jannatin tajree min tahtihaal-anharu yawma la yukhzee Allahu annabiyyawallatheena amanoo maAAahu nooruhum yasAAabayna aydeehim wabi-aymanihim yaqooloona rabbanaatmim lana noorana waghfir lanainnaka AAala kulli shay-in qadeer
66:8.translation: O you who have believed, repent to Allāh with sincere repentance. Perhaps your Lord will remove from you your misdeeds and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow [on] the Day when Allāh will not disgrace the Prophet and those who believed with him. Their light will proceed before them and on their right; they will say, "Our Lord, perfect for us our light and forgive us. Indeed, You are over all things competent."

66:9.transliteration: Ya ayyuha annabiyyu jahidialkuffara walmunafiqeena waghluthAAalayhim wama/wahum jahannamu wabi/sa almaseer
66:9.translation: O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be harsh upon them. And their refuge is Hell, and wretched is the destination.

66:10.transliteration: Daraba Allahu mathalan lillatheenakafaroo imraata noohin wamraata lootin kanatatahta AAabdayni min AAibadina salihaynifakhanatahuma falam yughniya AAanhumamina Allahi shay-an waqeela odkhula annaramaAAa addakhileen
66:10.translation: Allāh presents an example of those who disbelieved: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants but betrayed them, so they [i.e., those prophets] did not avail them from Allāh at all, and it was said, "Enter the Fire with those who enter."

66:11.transliteration: Wadaraba Allahu mathalan lillatheenaamanoo imraata firAAawna ith qalat rabbiibni lee AAindaka baytan fee aljannati wanajjinee min firAAawnawaAAamalihi wanajjinee mina alqawmi aththalimeen
66:11.translation: And Allāh presents an example of those who believed: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said, "My Lord, build for me near You a house in Paradise and save me from Pharaoh and his deeds and save me from the wrongdoing people."

66:12.transliteration: Wamaryama ibnata AAimrana allatee ahsanatfarjaha fanafakhna feehi min roohinawasaddaqat bikalimati rabbiha wakutubihi wakanatmina alqaniteen
66:12.translation: And [the example of] Mary, the daughter of ʿImrān, who guarded her chastity, so We blew into [her garment] through Our angel [i.e., Gabriel], and she believed in the words of her Lord and His scriptures and was of the devoutly obedient.

67:1.transliteration: Tabaraka allathee biyadihialmulku wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer
67:1.translation: Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent -

67:2.transliteration: Allathee khalaqa almawta walhayataliyabluwakum ayyukum ahsanu AAamalan wahuwa alAAazeezualghafoor
67:2.translation: [He] who created death and life to test you [as to] which of you is best in deed - and He is the Exalted in Might, the Forgiving -

67:3.transliteration: Allathee khalaqa sabAAa samawatintibaqan ma tara fee khalqi arrahmanimin tafawutin farjiAAi albasara hal taramin futoor
67:3.translation: [And] who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return [your] vision [to the sky]; do you see any breaks?

67:4.transliteration: Thumma irjiAAi albasara karratayniyanqalib ilayka albasaru khasi-an wahuwa haseer
67:4.translation: Then return [your] vision twice again. [Your] vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.

67:5.transliteration: Walaqad zayyanna assamaaaddunya bimasabeeha wajaAAalnaharujooman lishshayateeni waaAAtadna lahumAAathaba assaAAeer
67:5.translation: And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with lamps [i.e., stars] and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils and have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

67:6.transliteration: Walillatheena kafaroo birabbihim AAathabujahannama wabi/sa almaseer
67:6.translation: And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.

67:7.transliteration: Itha olqoo feeha samiAAoo lahashaheeqan wahiya tafoor
67:7.translation: When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a [dreadful] inhaling while it boils up.

67:8.transliteration: Takadu tamayyazu mina alghaythikullama olqiya feeha fawjun saalahum khazanatuhaalam ya/tikum natheer
67:8.translation: It almost bursts with rage. Every time a company is thrown into it, its keepers ask them, "Did there not come to you a warner?"

67:9.transliteration: Qaloo bala qad jaananatheerun fakaththabna waqulna manazzala Allahu min shay-in in antum illa fee dalalinkabeer
67:9.translation: They will say, "Yes, a warner had come to us, but we denied and said, 'Allāh has not sent down anything. You are not but in great error.'"

67:10.transliteration: Waqaloo law kunna nasmaAAu awnaAAqilu ma kunna fee as-habi assaAAeer
67:10.translation: And they will say, "If only we had been listening or reasoning, we would not be among the companions of the Blaze."

67:11.translation: And they will admit their sin, so [it is] alienation for the companions of the Blaze.

67:12.transliteration: Inna allatheena yakhshawna rabbahumbilghaybi lahum maghfiratun waajrun kabeer
67:12.translation: Indeed, those who fear their Lord unseen will have forgiveness and great reward.

67:13.transliteration: Waasirroo qawlakum awi ijharoo bihi innahuAAaleemun bithati assudoor
67:13.translation: And conceal your speech or publicize it; indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

67:14.transliteration: Ala yaAAlamu man khalaqa wahuwa allateefualkhabeer
67:14.translation: Does He who created not know, while He is the Subtle, the Aware?

67:15.transliteration: Huwa allathee jaAAala lakumu al-ardathaloolan famshoo fee manakibihawakuloo min rizqihi wa-ilayhi annushoor
67:15.translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision - and to Him is the resurrection.

67:16.transliteration: Aamintum man fee assama-i anyakhsifa bikumu al-arda fa-itha hiya tamoor
67:16.translation: Do you feel secure that He who is above would not cause the earth to swallow you and suddenly it would sway?

67:17.transliteration: Am amintum man fee assama-ian yursila AAalaykum hasiban fasataAAlamoona kayfa natheer
67:17.translation: Or do you feel secure that He who is above would not send against you a storm of stones? Then you would know how [severe] was My warning.

67:18.transliteration: Walaqad kaththaba allatheenamin qablihim fakayfa kana nakeer
67:18.translation: And already had those before them denied, and how [terrible] was My reproach.

67:19.transliteration: Awa lam yaraw ila attayrifawqahum saffatin wayaqbidna mayumsikuhunna illa arrahmanu innahubikulli shay-in baseer
67:19.translation: Do they not see the birds above them with wings outspread and [sometimes] folded in? None holds them [aloft] except the Most Merciful. Indeed He is, of all things, Seeing.

67:20.transliteration: Amman hatha allathee huwajundun lakum yansurukum min dooni arrahmaniini alkafiroona illa fee ghuroor
67:20.translation: Or who is it that could be an army for you to aid you other than the Most Merciful? The disbelievers are not but in delusion.

67:21.transliteration: Amman hatha allatheeyarzuqukum in amsaka rizqahu bal lajjoo fee AAutuwwin wanufoor
67:21.translation: Or who is it that could provide for you if He withheld His provision? But they have persisted in insolence and aversion.

67:22.transliteration: Afaman yamshee mukibban AAala wajhihiahda amman yamshee sawiyyan AAala siratinmustaqeem
67:22.translation: Then is one who walks fallen on his face better guided or one who walks erect on a straight path?

67:23.transliteration: Qul huwa allathee anshaakum wajaAAalalakumu assamAAa wal-absara wal-af-idataqaleelan ma tashkuroon
67:23.translation: Say, "It is He who has produced you and made for you hearing and vision and hearts [i.e., intellect]; little are you grateful."

67:24.transliteration: Qul huwa allathee tharaakumfee al-ardi wa-ilayhi tuhsharoon
67:24.translation: Say, "It is He who has multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered."

67:25.transliteration: Wayaqooloona mata hathaalwaAAdu in kuntum sadiqeen
67:25.translation: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"

67:26.transliteration: Qul innama alAAilmu AAinda Allahiwa-innama ana natheerun mubeen
67:26.translation: Say, "The knowledge is only with Allāh, and I am only a clear warner."

67:27.transliteration: Falamma raawhu zulfatan see-atwujoohu allatheena kafaroo waqeela hatha allatheekuntum bihi taddaAAoon
67:27.translation: But when they see it approaching, the faces of those who disbelieve will be distressed, and it will be said, "This is that for which you used to call."

67:28.transliteration: Qul araaytum in ahlakaniya Allahuwaman maAAiya aw rahimana faman yujeeru alkafireenamin AAathabin aleem
67:28.translation: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Have you considered: whether Allāh should cause my death and those with me or have mercy upon us, who can protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?"

67:29.transliteration: Qul huwa arrahmanu amannabihi waAAalayhi tawakkalna fasataAAlamoona man huwa fee dalalinmubeen
67:29.translation: Say, "He is the Most Merciful; we have believed in Him, and upon Him we have relied. And you will [come to] know who it is that is in clear error."

67:30.transliteration: Qul araaytum in asbaha maokumghawran faman ya/teekum bima-in maAAeen
67:30.translation: Say, "Have you considered: if your water was to become sunken [into the earth], then who could bring you flowing water?"

68:1.translation: Nūn. By the pen and what they inscribe,

68:2.translation: You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.

68:3.translation: And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.

68:4.translation: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.

68:5.translation: So you will see and they will see.

68:6.translation: Which of you is the afflicted [by a devil].

68:7.transliteration: Inna rabbaka huwa aAAlamu biman dallaAAan sabeelihi wahuwa aAAlamu bilmuhtadeen
68:7.translation: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

68:8.translation: Then do not obey the deniers.

68:9.translation: They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].

68:10.translation: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer

68:11.translation: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -

68:12.translation: A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,

68:13.translation: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

68:14.translation: Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

68:15.transliteration: Itha tutla AAalayhi ayatunaqala asateeru al-awwaleen
68:15.translation: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

68:16.translation: We will brand him upon the snout.

68:17.transliteration: Inna balawnahum kamabalawna as-haba aljannati ith aqsamoolayasrimunnaha musbiheen
68:17.translation: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

68:19.transliteration: Fatafa AAalayha ta-ifunmin rabbika wahum na-imoon
68:19.translation: So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.

68:20.translation: And it became as though reaped.

68:21.translation: And they called one another at morning,

68:22.transliteration: Ani ighdoo AAala harthikum inkuntum sarimeen
68:22.translation: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."

68:23.translation: So they set out, while lowering their voices,

68:24.translation: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."

68:25.translation: And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.

68:26.translation: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;

68:28.transliteration: Qala awsatuhum alam aqul lakumlawla tusabbihoon
68:28.translation: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

68:29.translation: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

68:30.translation: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.

68:31.translation: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.

68:32.transliteration: AAasa rabbuna an yubdilanakhayran minha inna ila rabbina raghiboon
68:32.translation: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

68:33.transliteration: Kathalika alAAathabu walaAAathabual-akhirati akbaru law kanoo yaAAlamoon
68:33.translation: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.

68:34.translation: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.

68:35.translation: Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?

68:36.translation: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?

68:37.translation: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn

68:38.translation: That indeed for you is whatever you choose?

68:39.transliteration: Am lakum aymanun AAalayna balighatunila yawmi alqiyamati inna lakum lama tahkumoon
68:39.translation: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?

68:40.translation: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

68:41.transliteration: Am lahum shurakao falya/too bishuraka-ihimin kanoo sadiqeen
68:41.translation: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.

68:42.transliteration: Yawma yukshafu AAan saqin wayudAAawnaila assujoodi fala yastateeAAoon
68:42.translation: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

68:43.transliteration: KhashiAAatan absaruhumtarhaquhum thillatun waqad kanoo yudAAawna ilaassujoodi wahum salimoon
68:43.translation: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

68:44.transliteration: Fatharnee waman yukaththibubihatha alhadeethi sanastadrijuhum min haythula yaAAlamoon
68:44.translation: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.

68:45.translation: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.

68:46.transliteration: Am tas-aluhum ajran fahum min maghraminmuthqaloon
68:46.translation: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

68:47.translation: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

68:48.transliteration: Fasbir lihukmi rabbikawala takun kasahibi alhooti ith nadawahuwa makthoom
68:48.translation: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

68:49.transliteration: Lawla an tadarakahu niAAmatunmin rabbihi lanubitha bilAAara-i wahuwa mathmoom
68:49.translation: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

68:50.translation: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.

68:51.transliteration: Wa-in yakadu allatheenakafaroo layuzliqoonaka bi-absarihim lamma samiAAooaththikra wayaqooloona innahu lamajnoon
68:51.translation: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

68:52.translation: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.

69:3.translation: And what can make you know what is the Inevitable Reality?

69:4.translation: Thamūd and ʿAad denied the Striking Calamity [i.e., the Resurrection].

69:5.translation: So as for Thamūd, they were destroyed by the overpowering [blast].

69:6.translation: And as for ʿAad, they were destroyed by a screaming, violent wind

69:7.transliteration: Sakhkharaha AAalayhim sabAAa layalinwathamaniyata ayyamin husooman fataraalqawma feeha sarAAa kaannahum aAAjazunakhlin khawiya
69:7.translation: Which He [i.e., Allāh] imposed upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so you would see the people therein fallen as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.

69:8.translation: Then do you see of them any remains?

69:9.translation: And there came Pharaoh and those before him and the overturned cities with sin.

69:10.translation: And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a seizure exceeding [in severity].

69:11.transliteration: Inna lamma taghaalmao hamalnakum fee aljariya
69:11.translation: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried you [i.e., your ancestors] in the sailing ship

69:12.translation: That We might make it for you a reminder and [that] a conscious ear would be conscious of it.

69:13.translation: Then when the Horn is blown with one blast

69:14.translation: And the earth and the mountains are lifted and leveled with one blow [i.e., stroke] -

69:15.translation: Then on that Day, the Occurrence [i.e., Resurrection] will occur,

69:16.translation: And the heaven will split [open], for that Day it is infirm.

69:17.transliteration: Walmalaku AAala arja-ihawayahmilu AAarsha rabbika fawqahum yawma-ithin thamaniya
69:17.translation: And the angels are at its edges. And there will bear the Throne of your Lord above them, that Day, eight [of them].

69:18.translation: That Day, you will be exhibited [for judgement]; not hidden among you is anything concealed.

69:19.transliteration: Faamma man ootiya kitabahubiyameenihi fayaqoolu haomu iqraoo kitabiyah
69:19.translation: So as for he who is given his record in his right hand, he will say, "Here, read my record!

69:20.translation: Indeed, I was certain that I would be meeting my account."

69:21.translation: So he will be in a pleasant life -

69:23.translation: Its [fruit] to be picked hanging near.

69:24.transliteration: Kuloo washraboo hanee-an bimaaslaftum fee al-ayyami alkhaliya
69:24.translation: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you put forth in the days past."

69:25.transliteration: Waamma man ootiya kitabahubishimalihi fayaqoolu ya laytanee lam oota kitabiyah
69:25.translation: But as for he who is given his record in his left hand, he will say, "Oh, I wish I had not been given my record

69:26.translation: And had not known what is my account.

69:27.translation: I wish it [i.e., my death] had been the decisive one.

69:28.translation: My wealth has not availed me.

69:29.translation: Gone from me is my authority."

69:30.translation: [Allāh will say], "Seize him and shackle him.

69:32.transliteration: Thumma fee silsilatin tharAAuhasabAAoona thiraAAan faslukooh
69:32.translation: Then into a chain whose length is seventy cubits insert him."

69:33.translation: Indeed, he did not used to believe in Allāh, the Most Great,

69:34.translation: Nor did he encourage the feeding of the poor.

69:35.translation: So there is not for him here this Day any devoted friend

69:36.translation: Nor any food except from the discharge of wounds;

69:37.translation: None will eat it except the sinners.

69:38.translation: So I swear by what you see

69:39.translation: And what you do not see

69:40.translation: [That] indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is the word of a noble Messenger.

69:41.transliteration: Wama huwa biqawli shaAAirinqaleelan ma tu/minoon
69:41.translation: And it is not the word of a poet; little do you believe.

69:42.translation: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little do you remember.

69:43.translation: [It is] a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.

69:44.translation: And if he [i.e., Muḥammad] had made up about Us some [false] sayings,

69:45.translation: We would have seized him by the right hand;

69:46.translation: Then We would have cut from him the aorta.

69:47.transliteration: Fama minkum min ahadin AAanhu hajizeen
69:47.translation: And there is no one of you who could prevent [Us] from him.

69:48.translation: And indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is a reminder for the righteous.

69:49.translation: And indeed, We know that among you are deniers.

69:50.translation: And indeed, it will be [a cause of] regret upon the disbelievers.

69:51.translation: And indeed, it is the truth of certainty.

69:52.translation: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.

70:1.translation: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen

70:2.translation: To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.

70:3.translation: [It is] from Allāh, owner of the ways of ascent.

70:4.transliteration: TaAAruju almala-ikatu warroohuilayhi fee yawmin kana miqdaruhu khamseena alfasana
70:4.translation: The angels and the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.

70:5.translation: So be patient with gracious patience.

70:8.translation: On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,

70:9.translation: And the mountains will be like wool,

70:10.translation: And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,

70:11.transliteration: Yubassaroonahum yawaddu almujrimu lawyaftadee min AAathabi yawmi-ithin bibaneeh
70:11.translation: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children.

70:12.translation: And his wife and his brother

70:13.translation: And his nearest kindred who shelter him.

70:14.translation: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.

70:15.translation: No! Indeed, it is the Flame [of Hell],

70:17.translation: It invites he who turned his back [on truth] and went away [from obedience]

70:21.translation: And when good touches him, withholding [of it],

70:23.translation: Those who are constant in their prayer

70:24.translation: And those within whose wealth is a known right

70:25.translation: For the petitioner and the deprived -

70:26.translation: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense

70:27.translation: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -

70:28.translation: Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -

70:29.translation: And those who guard their private parts

70:30.transliteration: Illa AAala azwajihim awma malakat aymanuhum fa-innahum ghayru maloomeen
70:30.translation: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -

70:31.transliteration: Famani ibtagha waraa thalikafaola-ika humu alAAadoon
70:31.translation: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -

70:32.translation: And those who are to their trusts and promises attentive.

70:33.translation: And those who are in their testimonies upright

70:34.translation: And those who [carefully] maintain their prayer:

70:35.translation: They will be in gardens, honored.

70:36.translation: So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muḥammad],

70:37.translation: [To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?

70:38.transliteration: AyatmaAAu kullu imri-in minhum anyudkhala jannata naAAeem
70:38.translation: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?

70:39.translation: No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.

70:40.transliteration: Fala oqsimu birabbi almashariqiwalmagharibi inna laqadiroon
70:40.translation: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able

70:41.transliteration: AAala an nubaddila khayran minhum wamanahnu bimasbooqeen
70:41.translation: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.

70:42.transliteration: Fatharhum yakhoodoowayalAAaboo hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allatheeyooAAadoon
70:42.translation: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

70:43.transliteration: Yawma yakhrujoona mina al-ajdathi siraAAankaannahum ila nusubin yoofidoon
70:43.translation: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

70:44.transliteration: KhashiAAatan absaruhumtarhaquhum thillatun thalika alyawmu allatheekanoo yooAAadoon
70:44.translation: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

71:1.transliteration: Inna arsalna noohan ilaqawmihi an anthir qawmaka min qabli an ya/tiyahum AAathabunaleem
71:1.translation: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful punishment."

71:2.transliteration: Qala ya qawmi innee lakum natheerunmubeen
71:2.translation: He said, "O my people, indeed I am to you a clear warner -

71:3.translation: To worship Allāh, fear Him and obey me.

71:4.transliteration: Yaghfir lakum min thunoobikumwayu-akhkhirkum ila ajalin musamman inna ajala Allahiitha jaa la yu-akhkharu law kuntumtaAAlamoon
71:4.translation: He [i.e., Allāh] will forgive you of your sins and delay you for a specified term. Indeed, the time [set by] Allāh, when it comes, will not be delayed, if you only knew."

71:5.transliteration: Qala rabbi innee daAAawtu qawmeelaylan wanahara
71:5.translation: He said, "My Lord, indeed I invited my people [to truth] night and day.

71:6.translation: But my invitation increased them not except in flight [i.e., aversion].

71:7.transliteration: Wa-innee kullama daAAawtuhumlitaghfira lahum jaAAaloo asabiAAahum fee athanihimwastaghshaw thiyabahum waasarroo wastakbarooistikbara
71:7.translation: And indeed, every time I invited them that You may forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, covered themselves with their garments, persisted, and were arrogant with [great] arrogance.

71:9.transliteration: Thumma innee aAAlantu lahum waasrartu lahumisrara
71:9.translation: Then I announced to them and [also] confided to them secretly

71:10.translation: And said, 'Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver.

71:11.translation: He will send [rain from] the sky upon you in [continuing] showers

71:12.transliteration: Wayumdidkum bi-amwalin wabaneenawayajAAal lakum jannatin wayajAAal lakum anhara
71:12.translation: And give you increase in wealth and children and provide for you gardens and provide for you rivers.

71:13.translation: What is [the matter] with you that you do not attribute to Allāh [due] grandeur

71:14.translation: While He has created you in stages?

71:15.transliteration: Alam taraw kayfa khalaqa AllahusabAAa samawatin tibaqa
71:15.translation: Do you not consider how Allāh has created seven heavens in layers

71:16.transliteration: WajaAAala alqamara feehinna nooran wajaAAalaashshamsa siraja
71:16.translation: And made the moon therein a [reflected] light and made the sun a burning lamp?

71:17.translation: And Allāh has caused you to grow from the earth a [progressive] growth.

71:18.translation: Then He will return you into it and extract you [another] extraction.

71:19.translation: And Allāh has made for you the earth an expanse

71:20.translation: That you may follow therein roads of passage.'"

71:21.transliteration: Qala noohun rabbi innahum AAasawneewattabaAAoo man lam yazidhu maluhu wawaladuhu illakhasara
71:21.translation: Noah said, "My Lord, indeed they have disobeyed me and followed him whose wealth and children will not increase him except in loss.

71:22.translation: And they conspired an immense conspiracy

71:23.transliteration: Waqaloo la tatharunna alihatakumwala tatharunna waddan wala suwaAAanwala yaghootha wayaAAooqa wanasra
71:23.translation: And said, 'Never leave your gods and never leave Wadd or Suwāʿ or Yaghūth and Yaʿūq and Nasr.'

71:24.transliteration: Waqad adalloo katheeran walatazidi aththalimeena illa dalala
71:24.translation: And already they have misled many. And, [my Lord], do not increase the wrongdoers except in error."

71:25.transliteration: Mimma khatee-atihimoghriqoo faodkhiloo naran falam yajidoo lahum min dooniAllahi ansara
71:25.translation: Because of their sins they were drowned and put into the Fire, and they found not for themselves besides Allāh [any] helpers.

71:26.transliteration: Waqala noohun rabbi latathar AAala al-ardi mina alkafireenadayyara
71:26.translation: And Noah said, "My Lord, do not leave upon the earth from among the disbelievers an inhabitant.

71:27.transliteration: Innaka in tatharhum yudillooAAibadaka wala yalidoo illa fajirankaffara
71:27.translation: Indeed, if You leave them, they will mislead Your servants and not beget except [every] wicked one and [confirmed] disbeliever.

71:28.transliteration: Rabbi ighfir lee waliwalidayyawaliman dakhala baytiya mu/minan walilmu/mineena walmu/minatiwala tazidi aththalimeena illatabaran
71:28.translation: My Lord, forgive me and my parents and whoever enters my house a believer and the believing men and believing women. And do not increase the wrongdoers except in destruction."

72:1.transliteration: Qul oohiya ilayya annahu istamaAAanafarun mina aljinni faqaloo inna samiAAnaqur-anan AAajaba
72:1.translation: Say, [O Muḥammad], "It has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened and said, 'Indeed, we have heard an amazing Qur’ān [i.e., recitation].

72:2.transliteration: Yahdee ila arrushdi faamannabihi walan nushrika birabbina ahada
72:2.translation: It guides to the right course, and we have believed in it. And we will never associate with our Lord anyone.

72:3.transliteration: Waannahu taAAala jaddu rabbinama ittakhatha sahibatan wala walada
72:3.translation: And [it teaches] that exalted is the nobleness of our Lord; He has not taken a wife or a son

72:4.transliteration: Waannahu kana yaqoolu safeehunaAAala Allahi shatata
72:4.translation: And that our foolish one [i.e., Iblees] has been saying about Allāh an excessive transgression.

72:5.transliteration: Waanna thanannaan lan taqoola al-insu waljinnu AAala Allahikathiba
72:5.translation: And we had thought that mankind and the jinn would never speak about Allāh a lie.

72:6.transliteration: Waannahu kana rijalun minaal-insi yaAAoothoona birijalin mina aljinni fazadoohumrahaqa
72:6.translation: And there were men from mankind who sought refuge in men from the jinn, so they [only] increased them in burden [i.e., sin].

72:7.transliteration: Waannahum thannoo kama thanantuman lan yabAAatha Allahu ahada
72:7.translation: And they had thought, as you thought, that Allāh would never send anyone [as a messenger].

72:8.transliteration: Waanna lamasna assamaafawajadnaha muli-at harasan shadeedanwashuhuba
72:8.translation: And we have sought [to reach] the heaven but found it filled with powerful guards and burning flames.

72:9.transliteration: Waanna kunna naqAAudu minhamaqaAAida lissamAAi faman yastamiAAi al-anayajid lahu shihaban rasada
72:9.translation: And we used to sit therein in positions for hearing, but whoever listens now will find a burning flame lying in wait for him.

72:10.transliteration: Waanna la nadree asharrunoreeda biman fee al-ardi am arada bihim rabbuhumrashada
72:10.translation: And we do not know [therefore] whether evil is intended for those on earth or whether their Lord intends for them a right course.

72:11.transliteration: Waanna minna assalihoonawaminna doona thalika kunna tara-iqaqidada
72:11.translation: And among us are the righteous, and among us are [others] not so; we were [of] divided ways.

72:12.transliteration: Wanna thanannaan lan nuAAjiza Allaha fee al-ardi walan nuAAjizahuharaba
72:12.translation: And we have become certain that we will never cause failure to Allāh upon earth, nor can we escape Him by flight.

72:13.transliteration: Waanna lamma samiAAnaalhuda amanna bihi faman yu/min birabbihifala yakhafu bakhsan wala rahaqa
72:13.translation: And when we heard the guidance [i.e., the Qur’ān], we believed in it. And whoever believes in his Lord will not fear deprivation or burden.

72:14.transliteration: Waanna minna almuslimoonawaminna alqasitoona faman aslama faola-ikataharraw rashada
72:14.translation: And among us are Muslims [in submission to Allāh], and among us are the unjust. And whoever has become Muslim - those have sought out the right course.

72:15.translation: But as for the unjust, they will be, for Hell, firewood.'

72:16.transliteration: Waallawi istaqamoo AAala attareeqatilaasqaynahum maan ghadaqa
72:16.translation: And [Allāh revealed] that if they had remained straight on the way, We would have given them abundant rain [i.e., provision].

72:17.transliteration: Linaftinahum feehi waman yuAArid AAanthikri rabbihi yasluk-hu AAathaban saAAada
72:17.translation: So We might test them therein. And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord He will put into arduous punishment.

72:18.transliteration: Waanna almasajida lillahi falatadAAoo maAAa Allahi ahada
72:18.translation: And [He revealed] that the masjids are for Allāh, so do not invoke with Allāh anyone.

72:19.transliteration: Waannahu lamma qama AAabdu AllahiyadAAoohu kadoo yakoonoona AAalayhi libada
72:19.translation: And that when the Servant [i.e., Prophet] of Allāh stood up supplicating Him, they almost became about him a compacted mass."

72:20.transliteration: Qul innama adAAoo rabbee walaoshriku bihi ahada
72:20.translation: Say, [O Muḥammad], "I only invoke my Lord and do not associate with Him anyone."

72:21.transliteration: Qul innee la amliku lakum darranwala rashada
72:21.translation: Say, "Indeed, I do not possess for you [the power of] harm or right direction."

72:22.transliteration: Qul innee lan yujeeranee mina Allahiahadun walan ajida min doonihi multahada
72:22.translation: Say, "Indeed, there will never protect me from Allāh anyone [if I should disobey], nor will I find in other than Him a refuge.

72:23.transliteration: Illa balaghan mina Allahiwarisalatihi waman yaAAsi Allahawarasoolahu fa-inna lahu nara jahannama khalideenafeeha abada
72:23.translation: But [I have for you] only notification from Allāh, and His messages." And whoever disobeys Allāh and His Messenger - then indeed, for him is the fire of Hell; they will abide therein forever.

72:24.transliteration: Hatta itha raaw mayooAAadoona fasayaAAlamoona man adAAafu nasiranwaaqallu AAadada
72:24.translation: [The disbelievers continue] until, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who is weaker in helpers and less in number.

72:25.transliteration: Qul in adree aqareebun ma tooAAadoonaam yajAAalu lahu rabbee amada
72:25.translation: Say, "I do not know if what you are promised is near or if my Lord will grant for it a [long] period."

72:26.transliteration: AAalimu alghaybi fala yuthhiruAAala ghaybihi ahada
72:26.translation: [He is] Knower of the unseen, and He does not disclose His [knowledge of the] unseen to anyone

72:27.transliteration: Illa mani irtada min rasoolinfa-innahu yasluku min bayni yadayhi wamin khalfihi rasada
72:27.translation: Except whom He has approved of messengers, and indeed, He sends before him [i.e., each messenger] and behind him observers

72:28.transliteration: LiyaAAlama an qad ablaghoo risalatirabbihim waahata bima ladayhim waahsa kullashay-in AAadada
72:28.translation: That he [i.e., Muḥammad (ﷺ)] may know that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord; and He has encompassed whatever is with them and has enumerated all things in number.

73:1.translation: O you who wraps himself [in clothing]

73:2.translation: Arise [to pray] the night, except for a little -

73:3.translation: Half of it - or subtract from it a little

73:4.translation: Or add to it, and recite the Qur’ān with measured recitation.

73:5.translation: Indeed, We will cast upon you a heavy word.

73:6.transliteration: Inna nashi-ata allayli hiya ashaddu wat-anwaaqwamu qeela
73:6.translation: Indeed, the hours of the night are more effective for concurrence [of heart and tongue] and more suitable for words.

73:7.translation: Indeed, for you by day is prolonged occupation.

73:8.translation: And remember the name of your Lord and devote yourself to Him with [complete] devotion.

73:9.transliteration: Rabbu almashriqi walmaghribi lailaha illa huwa fattakhithhu wakeela
73:9.translation: [He is] the Lord of the East and the West; there is no deity except Him, so take Him as Disposer of [your] affairs.

73:10.transliteration: Wasbir AAala mayaqooloona wahjurhum hajran jameela
73:10.translation: And be patient over what they say and avoid them with gracious avoidance.

73:11.translation: And leave Me with [the matter of] the deniers, those of ease [in life], and allow them respite a little.

73:12.translation: Indeed, with Us [for them] are shackles and burning fire.

73:13.translation: And food that chokes and a painful punishment -

73:14.transliteration: Yawma tarjufu al-ardu waljibaluwakanati aljibalu katheeban maheela
73:14.translation: On the Day the earth and the mountains will convulse and the mountains will become a heap of sand pouring down.

73:15.transliteration: Inna arsalna ilaykum rasoolanshahidan AAalaykum kama arsalna ilafirAAawna rasoola
73:15.translation: Indeed, We have sent to you a Messenger as a witness upon you just as We sent to Pharaoh a messenger.

73:16.translation: But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a ruinous seizure.

73:17.transliteration: Fakayfa tattaqoona in kafartum yawmanyajAAalu alwildana sheeba
73:17.translation: Then how can you fear, if you disbelieve, a Day that will make the children white-haired?

73:18.translation: The heaven will break apart therefrom; ever is His promise fulfilled.

73:19.transliteration: Inna hathihi tathkiratun famanshaa ittakhatha ila rabbihi sabeela
73:19.translation: Indeed, this is a reminder, so whoever wills may take to his Lord a way.

73:20.transliteration: Inna rabbaka yaAAlamu annaka taqoomu adnamin thuluthayi allayli wanisfahu wathuluthahu wata-ifatunmina allatheena maAAaka wallahu yuqaddiruallayla wannahara AAalima an lan tuhsoohufataba AAalaykum faqraoo ma tayassara minaalqur-ani AAalima an sayakoonu minkum marda waakharoonayadriboona fee al-ardi yabtaghoona min fadliAllahi waakharoona yuqatiloona fee sabeeliAllahi faqraoo ma tayassara minhu waaqeemooassalata waatoo azzakatawaaqridoo Allaha qardan hasanan wamatuqaddimoo li-anfusikum min khayrin tajidoohu AAinda Allahihuwa khayran waaAAthama ajran wastaghfirooAllaha inna Allaha ghafoorun raheem
73:20.translation: Indeed, your Lord knows, [O Muḥammad], that you stand [in prayer] almost two thirds of the night or half of it or a third of it, and [so do] a group of those with you. And Allāh determines [the extent of] the night and the day. He has known that you [Muslims] will not be able to do it and has turned to you in forgiveness, so recite what is easy [for you] of the Qur’ān. He has known that there will be among you those who are ill and others traveling throughout the land seeking [something] of the bounty of Allāh and others fighting for the cause of Allāh. So recite what is easy from it and establish prayer and give zakāh and loan Allāh a goodly loan. And whatever good you put forward for yourselves - you will find it with Allāh. It is better and greater in reward. And seek forgiveness of Allāh. Indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

74:1.translation: O you who covers himself [with a garment],

74:6.translation: And do not confer favor to acquire more.

74:7.translation: But for your Lord be patient.

74:8.translation: And when the trumpet is blown,

74:9.translation: That Day will be a difficult day

74:11.translation: Leave Me with the one I created alone

74:12.translation: And to whom I granted extensive wealth

74:14.translation: And spread [everything] before him, easing [his life].

74:15.translation: Then he desires that I should add more.

74:16.translation: No! Indeed, he has been toward Our verses obstinate.

74:17.translation: I will cover him with arduous torment.

74:19.translation: So may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.

74:20.translation: Then may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.

74:23.translation: Then he turned back and was arrogant

74:24.translation: And said, "This is not but magic imitated [from others].

74:25.translation: This is not but the word of a human being."

74:26.translation: I will drive him into Saqar.

74:27.translation: And what can make you know what is Saqar?

74:28.translation: It lets nothing remain and leaves nothing [unburned],

74:31.transliteration: Wama jaAAalna as-habaannari illa mala-ikatan wamajaAAalna AAiddatahum illa fitnatan lillatheenakafaroo liyastayqina allatheena ootoo alkitabawayazdada allatheena amanoo eemananwala yartaba allatheena ootoo alkitabawalmu/minoona waliyaqoola allatheena feequloobihim maradun walkafiroona mathaarada Allahu bihatha mathalan kathalikayudillu Allahu man yashao wayahdee man yashaowama yaAAlamu junooda rabbika illa huwa wamahiya illa thikra lilbashar
74:31.translation: And We have not made the keepers of the Fire except angels. And We have not made their number except as a trial for those who disbelieve - that those who were given the Scripture will be convinced and those who have believed will increase in faith and those who were given the Scripture and the believers will not doubt and that those in whose hearts is disease [i.e., hypocrisy] and the disbelievers will say, "What does Allāh intend by this as an example?" Thus does Allāh send astray whom He wills and guide whom He wills. And none knows the soldiers of your Lord except Him. And it [i.e., mention of the Fire] is not but a reminder to humanity.

74:33.translation: And [by] the night when it departs.

74:34.translation: And [by] the morning when it brightens,

74:35.translation: Indeed, it [i.e., the Fire] is of the greatest [afflictions].

74:37.transliteration: Liman shaa minkum an yataqaddama awyataakhkhar
74:37.translation: To whoever wills among you to proceed or stay behind.

74:38.translation: Every soul, for what it has earned, will be retained.

74:39.translation: Except the companions of the right,.

74:40.translation: [Who will be] in gardens, questioning each other

74:42.translation: [And asking them], "What put you into Saqar?"

74:43.translation: They will say, "We were not of those who prayed,

74:44.translation: Nor did we used to feed the poor.

74:45.translation: And we used to enter into vain discourse with those who engaged [in it],

74:46.translation: And we used to deny the Day of Recompense

74:47.translation: Until there came to us the certainty [i.e., death]."

74:48.translation: So there will not benefit them the intercession of [any] intercessors.

74:49.translation: Then what is [the matter] with them that they are, from the reminder, turning away.

74:50.translation: As if they were alarmed donkeys.

74:52.transliteration: Bal yureedu kullu imri-in minhum an yu/tasuhufan munashshara
74:52.translation: Rather, every person among them desires that he would be given scriptures spread about.

74:53.translation: No! But they do not fear the Hereafter.

74:54.translation: No! Indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is a reminder.

74:55.translation: Then whoever wills will remember it.

74:56.transliteration: Wama yathkuroona illaan yashaa Allahu huwa ahlu attaqwawaahlu almaghfirat
74:56.translation: And they will not remember except that Allāh wills. He is worthy of fear and adequate for [granting] forgiveness.

75:1.translation: I swear by the Day of Resurrection

75:2.translation: And I swear by the reproaching soul [to the certainty of resurrection].

75:3.translation: Does man think that We will not assemble his bones?

75:4.translation: Yes. [We are] Able [even] to proportion his fingertips.

75:5.translation: But man desires to continue in sin.

75:6.translation: He asks, "When is the Day of Resurrection?"

75:9.translation: And the sun and the moon are joined,

75:10.translation: Man will say on that Day, "Where is the [place of] escape?"

75:12.translation: To your Lord, that Day, is the [place of] permanence.

75:13.translation: Man will be informed that Day of what he sent ahead and kept back.

75:14.translation: Rather, man, against himself, will be a witness,

75:15.translation: Even if he presents his excuses.

75:16.translation: Move not your tongue with it, [O Muḥammad], to hasten with it [i.e., recitation of the Qur’ān].

75:17.translation: Indeed, upon Us is its collection [in your heart] and [to make possible] its recitation.

75:18.translation: So when We have recited it [through Gabriel], then follow its recitation.

75:19.translation: Then upon Us is its clarification [to you].

75:20.translation: No! But you [i.e., mankind] love the immediate

75:22.translation: [Some] faces, that Day, will be radiant,

75:24.translation: And [some] faces, that Day, will be contorted,

75:25.translation: Expecting that there will be done to them [something] backbreaking.

75:26.translation: No! When it [i.e., the soul] has reached the collar bones

75:27.translation: And it is said, "Who will cure [him]?"

75:28.translation: And he [i.e., the dying one] is certain that it is the [time of] separation

75:29.translation: And the leg is wound about the leg,

75:30.translation: To your Lord, that Day, will be the procession.

75:31.translation: And he [i.e., the disbeliever] had not believed, nor had he prayed.

75:32.translation: But [instead], he denied and turned away.

75:33.translation: And then he went to his people, swaggering [in pride].

75:35.translation: Then woe to you, and woe!

75:36.translation: Does man think that he will be left neglected?

75:37.transliteration: Alam yaku nutfatan min manayyin yumna
75:37.translation: Had he not been a sperm from semen emitted?

75:38.translation: Then he was a clinging clot, and [Allāh] created [his form] and proportioned [him]

75:39.translation: And made of him two mates, the male and the female.

75:40.transliteration: Alaysa thalika biqadirin AAalaan yuhyiya almawta
75:40.translation: Is not that [Creator] Able to give life to the dead?

76:1.transliteration: Hal ata AAala al-insani heenunmina addahri lam yakun shay-an mathkoora
76:1.translation: Has there [not] come upon man a period of time when he was not a thing [even] mentioned?

76:2.transliteration: Inna khalaqna al-insanamin nutfatin amshajin nabtaleehi fajaAAalnahusameeAAan baseera
76:2.translation: Indeed, We created man from a sperm-drop mixture that We may try him; and We made him hearing and seeing.

76:3.transliteration: Inna hadaynahu assabeelaimma shakiran wa-imma kafoora
76:3.translation: Indeed, We guided him to the way, be he grateful or be he ungrateful.

76:4.translation: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains and shackles and a blaze.

76:5.transliteration: Inna al-abrara yashraboona min ka/sinkana mizajuha kafoora
76:5.translation: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of Kāfūr,

76:6.transliteration: AAaynan yashrabu biha AAibaduAllahi yufajjiroonaha tafjeera
76:6.translation: A spring of which the [righteous] servants of Allāh will drink; they will make it gush forth in force [and abundance].

76:7.transliteration: Yoofoona binnathri wayakhafoonayawman kana sharruhu mustateera
76:7.translation: They [are those who] fulfill [their] vows and fear a Day whose evil will be widespread.

76:8.transliteration: WayutAAimona attaAAamaAAala hubbihi miskeenan wayateeman waaseera
76:8.translation: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive,

76:9.transliteration: Innama nutAAimukum liwajhi Allahila nureedu minkum jazaan wala shukoora
76:9.translation: [Saying], "We feed you only for the face [i.e., approval] of Allāh. We wish not from you reward or gratitude.

76:10.transliteration: Inna nakhafu min rabbinayawman AAaboosan qamtareera
76:10.translation: Indeed, We fear from our Lord a Day austere and distressful."

76:11.transliteration: Fawaqahumu Allahu sharra thalikaalyawmi walaqqahum nadratan wasuroora
76:11.translation: So Allāh will protect them from the evil of that Day and give them radiance and happiness

76:12.translation: And will reward them for what they patiently endured [with] a garden [in Paradise] and silk [garments].

76:13.transliteration: Muttaki-eena feeha AAala al-ara-ikila yarawna feeha shamsan wala zamhareera
76:13.translation: [They will be] reclining therein on adorned couches. They will not see therein any [burning] sun or [freezing] cold.

76:14.translation: And near above them are its shades, and its [fruit] to be picked will be lowered in compliance.

76:15.transliteration: Wayutafu AAalayhim bi-aniyatinmin fiddatin waakwabin kanat qawareera
76:15.translation: And there will be circulated among them vessels of silver and cups having been [created] clear [as glass],

76:16.translation: Clear glasses [made] from silver of which they have determined the measure.

76:17.translation: And they will be given to drink a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of ginger

76:18.translation: [From] a fountain within it [i.e., Paradise] named Salsabeel.

76:19.transliteration: Wayatoofu AAalayhim wildanunmukhalladoona itha raaytahum hasibtahum lu/lu-anmanthoora
76:19.translation: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal. When you see them, you would think them [as beautiful as] scattered pearls.

76:20.transliteration: Wa-itha raayta thamma raaytanaAAeeman wamulkan kabeera
76:20.translation: And when you look there [in Paradise], you will see pleasure and great dominion.

76:21.transliteration: AAaliyahum thiyabu sundusinkhudrun wa-istabraqun wahulloo asawira minfiddatin wasaqahum rabbuhum sharaban tahoora
76:21.translation: Upon them [i.e., the inhabitants] will be green garments of fine silk and brocade. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink.

76:22.transliteration: Inna hatha kana lakum jazaanwakana saAAyukum mashkoora
76:22.translation: [And it will be said], "Indeed, this is for you a reward, and your effort has been appreciated."

76:23.translation: Indeed, it is We who have sent down to you, [O Muḥammad], the Qur’ān progressively.

76:24.transliteration: Fasbir lihukmi rabbikawala tutiAA minhum athiman aw kafoora
76:24.translation: So be patient for the decision of your Lord and do not obey from among them a sinner or ungrateful [disbeliever].

76:25.translation: And mention the name of your Lord [in prayer] morning and evening

76:26.transliteration: Wamina allayli fasjud lahu wasabbihhulaylan taweela
76:26.translation: And during the night prostrate to Him and exalt [i.e., praise] Him a long [part of the] night.

76:27.transliteration: Inna haola-i yuhibboonaalAAajilata wayatharoona waraahum yawmanthaqeela
76:27.translation: Indeed, these [disbelievers] love the immediate and leave behind them a grave Day.

76:28.transliteration: Nahnu khalaqnahum washadadnaasrahum wa-itha shi/na baddalna amthalahumtabdeela
76:28.translation: We have created them and strengthened their forms, and when We will, We can change their likenesses with [complete] alteration.

76:29.transliteration: Inna hathihi tathkiratun famanshaa ittakhatha ila rabbihi sabeela
76:29.translation: Indeed, this is a reminder, so he who wills may take to his Lord a way.

76:30.transliteration: Wama tashaoona illa anyashaa Allahu inna Allaha kanaAAaleeman hakeema
76:30.translation: And you do not will except that Allāh wills. Indeed, Allāh is ever Knowing and Wise.

76:31.transliteration: Yudkhilu man yashao fee rahmatihiwaththalimeena aAAadda lahum AAathabanaleema
76:31.translation: He admits whom He wills into His mercy; but the wrongdoers - He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

77:1.translation: By those [winds] sent forth in gusts

77:2.translation: And the winds that blow violently

77:5.translation: And those [angels] who deliver a message.

77:7.translation: Indeed, what you are promised is to occur.

77:8.translation: So when the stars are obliterated

77:9.translation: And when the heaven is opened

77:10.translation: And when the mountains are blown away

77:11.translation: And when the messengers' time has come...

77:12.translation: For what Day was it postponed?

77:14.translation: And what can make you know what is the Day of Judgement?

77:15.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:16.translation: Did We not destroy the former peoples?

77:17.translation: Then We will follow them with the later ones.

77:18.translation: Thus do We deal with the criminals.

77:19.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:20.translation: Did We not create you from a liquid disdained?

77:21.translation: And We placed it in a firm lodging [i.e., the womb]

77:23.translation: And We determined [it], and excellent [are We] to determine.

77:24.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:25.translation: Have We not made the earth a container

77:26.translation: Of the living and the dead?

77:27.transliteration: WajaAAalna feeha rawasiyashamikhatin waasqaynakum maan furata
77:27.translation: And We placed therein lofty, firmly set mountains and have given you to drink sweet water.

77:28.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:29.translation: [They will be told], "Proceed to that which you used to deny.

77:30.translation: Proceed to a shadow [of smoke] having three columns.

77:31.translation: [But having] no cool shade and availing not against the flame."

77:32.translation: Indeed, it throws sparks [as huge] as a fortress,

77:33.translation: As if they were yellowish [black] camels.

77:34.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:35.translation: This is a Day they will not speak,

77:36.translation: Nor will it be permitted for them to make an excuse.

77:37.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:38.translation: This is the Day of Judgement; We will have assembled you and the former peoples.

77:39.translation: So if you have a plan, then plan against Me.

77:40.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:41.translation: Indeed, the righteous will be among shades and springs

77:42.translation: And fruits from whatever they desire,

77:43.translation: [Being told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."

77:44.translation: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.

77:45.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:46.translation: [O disbelievers], eat and enjoy yourselves a little; indeed, you are criminals.

77:47.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:48.translation: And when it is said to them, "Bow [in prayer]," they do not bow.

77:49.translation: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.

77:50.translation: Then in what statement after it [i.e., the Qur’ān] will they believe?

78:1.translation: About what are they asking one another?

78:3.translation: That over which they are in disagreement.

78:4.translation: No! They are going to know.

78:5.translation: Then, no! They are going to know.

78:6.translation: Have We not made the earth a resting place?

78:8.translation: And We created you in pairs.

78:9.translation: And made your sleep [a means for] rest

78:10.translation: And made the night as clothing.

78:11.translation: And made the day for livelihood.

78:12.translation: And constructed above you seven strong [heavens].

78:14.translation: And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water.

78:15.translation: That We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation.

78:17.translation: Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -

78:18.translation: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes

78:19.translation: And the heaven is opened and will become gateways.

78:20.translation: And the mountains are removed and will be [but] a mirage.

78:21.translation: Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait

78:22.translation: For the transgressors, a place of return,

78:23.translation: In which they will remain for ages [unending].

78:24.translation: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink.

78:27.translation: Indeed, they were not expecting an account

78:28.translation: And denied Our verses with [emphatic] denial.

78:29.translation: But all things We have enumerated in writing.

78:30.translation: "So taste [the penalty], and never will We increase you except in torment."

78:31.translation: Indeed, for the righteous is attainment -

78:35.translation: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -

78:36.translation: [As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,

78:37.transliteration: Rabbi assamawati wal-ardiwama baynahuma arrahmani layamlikoona minhu khitaba
78:37.translation: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

78:38.transliteration: Yawma yaqoomu arroohu walmala-ikatusaffan la yatakallamoona illa man athinalahu arrahmanu waqala sawaba
78:38.translation: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

78:39.transliteration: Thalika alyawmu alhaqqu famanshaa ittakhatha ila rabbihi maaba
78:39.translation: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

78:40.transliteration: Inna antharnakum AAathabanqareeban yawma yanthuru almaro ma qaddamatyadahu wayaqoolu alkafiru ya laytanee kuntuturaba
78:40.translation: Indeed, We have warned you of an impending punishment on the Day when a man will observe what his hands have put forth and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish that I were dust!"

79:1.translation: By those [angels] who extract with violence

79:2.translation: And [by] those who remove with ease

79:3.translation: And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming

79:4.translation: And those who race each other in a race

79:6.translation: On the Day the blast [of the Horn] will convulse [creation],

79:7.translation: There will follow it the subsequent [one].

79:10.translation: They are [presently] saying, "Will we indeed be returned to [our] former state [of life]?

79:11.translation: Even if we should be decayed bones?"

79:12.translation: They say, "That, then, would be a losing return."

79:13.translation: Indeed, it will be but one shout,

79:14.translation: And suddenly they will be [alert] upon the earth's surface.

79:15.translation: Has there reached you the story of Moses? -

79:16.translation: When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā,

79:17.translation: "Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.

79:18.transliteration: Faqul hal laka ila an tazakka
79:18.translation: And say to him, 'Would you [be willing to] purify yourself

79:19.translation: And let me guide you to your Lord so you would fear [Him]?'"

79:20.translation: And he showed him the greatest sign,

79:21.translation: But he [i.e., Pharaoh] denied and disobeyed.

79:22.translation: Then he turned his back, striving [i.e., plotting].

79:23.translation: And he gathered [his people] and called out.

79:24.translation: And said, "I am your most exalted lord."

79:25.translation: So Allāh seized him in exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression]..

79:26.translation: Indeed in that is a lesson [i.e., warning] for whoever would fear [Allāh].

79:27.translation: Are you a more difficult creation or is the heaven? He [i.e., Allāh] constructed it.

79:28.translation: He raised its ceiling and proportioned it.

79:29.translation: And He darkened its night and extracted its brightness.

79:30.translation: And after that He spread the earth.

79:31.translation: He extracted from it its water and its pasture,

79:32.translation: And the mountains He set firmly

79:33.translation: As enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.

79:34.translation: But when there comes the greatest Overwhelming Calamity -

79:35.translation: The Day when man will remember that for which he strove,

79:36.translation: And Hellfire will be exposed for [all] those who see -

79:37.translation: So as for he who transgressed

79:38.translation: And preferred the life of the world,

79:39.translation: Then indeed, Hellfire will be [his] refuge.

79:40.transliteration: Waama man khafa maqamarabbihi wanaha annafsa AAani alhawa
79:40.translation: But as for he who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from [unlawful] inclination,

79:41.translation: Then indeed, Paradise will be [his] refuge.

79:42.translation: They ask you, [O Muḥammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival?

79:43.translation: In what [position] are you that you should mention it?

79:44.translation: To your Lord is its finality.

79:45.translation: You are only a warner for those who fear it.

79:46.transliteration: Kaannahum yawma yarawnaha lamyalbathoo illa AAashiyyatan aw duhaha
79:46.translation: It will be, on the Day they see it, as though they had not remained [in the world] except for an afternoon or a morning thereof.

80:1.translation: He [i.e., the Prophet (ﷺ) ] frowned and turned away

80:2.translation: Because there came to him the blind man, [interrupting].

80:3.translation: But what would make you perceive, [O Muḥammad], that perhaps he might be purified

80:4.translation: Or be reminded and the remembrance would benefit him?

80:5.translation: As for he who thinks himself without need,

80:7.translation: And not upon you [is any blame] if he will not be purified.

80:8.translation: But as for he who came to you striving [for knowledge]

80:11.translation: No! Indeed, they [i.e., these verses] are a reminder;

80:12.translation: So whoever wills may remember it.

80:17.translation: Destroyed [i.e., cursed] is man; how disbelieving is he.

80:18.translation: From what thing [i.e., substance] did He create him?

80:19.translation: From a sperm-drop He created him and destined for him;

80:20.translation: Then He eased the way for him;

80:21.translation: Then He causes his death and provides a grave for him.

80:22.translation: Then when He wills, He will resurrect him.

80:23.translation: No! He [i.e., man] has not yet accomplished what He commanded him.

80:24.translation: Then let mankind look at his food -

80:25.translation: How We poured down water in torrents,

80:26.translation: Then We broke open the earth, splitting [it with sprouts],

80:27.translation: And caused to grow within it grain

80:32.translation: [As] enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.

80:33.translation: But when there comes the Deafening Blast

80:34.translation: On the Day a man will flee from his brother

80:35.translation: And his mother and his father

80:36.translation: And his wife and his children,

80:37.translation: For every man, that Day, will be a matter adequate for him.

80:38.translation: [Some] faces, that Day, will be bright -

80:40.translation: And [other] faces, that Day, will have upon them dust.

80:42.translation: Those are the disbelievers, the wicked ones.

81:1.translation: When the sun is wrapped up [in darkness]

81:2.translation: And when the stars fall, dispersing,

81:3.translation: And when the mountains are removed

81:4.translation: And when full-term she-camels are neglected

81:5.translation: And when the wild beasts are gathered

81:6.translation: And when the seas are filled with flame

81:7.translation: And when the souls are paired

81:8.translation: And when the girl [who was] buried alive is asked

81:9.translation: For what sin she was killed

81:10.translation: And when the pages are spread [i.e., made public]

81:11.translation: And when the sky is stripped away

81:12.translation: And when Hellfire is set ablaze

81:13.translation: And when Paradise is brought near,

81:14.translation: A soul will [then] know what it has brought [with it].

81:15.translation: So I swear by the retreating stars -

81:16.translation: Those that run [their courses] and disappear [i.e., set]

81:17.translation: And by the night as it closes in

81:18.translation: And by the dawn when it breathes [i.e., stirs]

81:19.translation: [That] indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is a word [conveyed by] a noble messenger [i.e., Gabriel]

81:20.translation: [Who is] possessed of power and with the Owner of the Throne, secure [in position],

81:21.translation: Obeyed there [in the heavens] and trustworthy.

81:22.translation: And your companion [i.e., Prophet Muḥammad (ﷺ) ] is not [at all] mad.

81:23.translation: And he has already seen him [i.e., Gabriel] in the clear horizon.

81:24.translation: And he [i.e., Muḥammad (ﷺ)] is not a withholder of [knowledge of] the unseen.

81:25.translation: And it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is not the word of a devil, expelled [from the heavens].

81:27.translation: It is not except a reminder to the worlds

81:28.translation: For whoever wills among you to take a right course.

81:29.transliteration: Wama tashaoona illa anyashaa Allahu rabbu alAAalameen
81:29.translation: And you do not will except that Allāh wills - Lord of the worlds.

82:2.translation: And when the stars fall, scattering,

82:3.translation: And when the seas are erupted

82:4.translation: And when the [contents of] graves are scattered [i.e., exposed],

82:5.translation: A soul will [then] know what it has put forth and kept back.

82:6.transliteration: Ya ayyuha al-insanu magharraka birabbika alkareem
82:6.translation: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous,

82:7.translation: Who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you?

82:8.translation: In whatever form He willed has He assembled you.

82:9.translation: No! But you deny the Recompense.

82:10.translation: And indeed, [appointed] over you are keepers,

82:13.translation: Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure,

82:14.translation: And indeed, the wicked will be in Hellfire.

82:15.translation: They will [enter to] burn therein on the Day of Recompense,
